Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_10
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments53

Summary

The homeowner called Arizona Roofers to ask about certified roofing installation and pricing because their shingle roof is leaking and they want an estimate to potentially replace it. The representative gathered contact details, confirmed the caller is the homeowner, obtained the property address, email for sending a written proposal, and permission to send text updates. The homeowner stated the roof is about 20 years old (originally described as a 30-year roof), has no solar panels (but does have an air conditioning unit on the roof), and was unsure whether the property is in an HOA. The rep reassured the customer that leaking after 15–20 years is normal for shingle roofs and that their team is certified and established, then scheduled an on-site appointment for Thursday from 1–3 PM with confirmation and pre-arrival communication promised.

• Customer reports a leaking shingle roof and wants an estimate for replacing the roof.
• Roof age discussed as around 20 years old (customer referenced it as a 30-year roof originally).
• No solar panels; customer mentioned an air conditioning unit on the roof; HOA status was uncertain.

Action Items

☑ Dispatch a technician to the property for the scheduled on-site evaluation/estimate (Thursday, 1–3 PM).
☑ Send confirmation text updates and deliver the written proposal to the provided Yahoo email after the visit.
☑ Send a certified technician to inspect the roof leak and provide recommendations during the scheduled visit

Next Steps

➤ Technician to contact the customer by text/phone about 30 minutes before arrival on the appointment day.
➤ Technician to inspect the roof leak and provide recommendations and a written replacement estimate/proposal.
Sentiment
72%
Summary Confidence

SOP Compliance

72%

Greeting & IdentificationScheduling (basic)Close & Confirmation (partial)Needs Discovery (depth)Qualifying (BANT)Setting Expectations (depth)Objection Handling (quality)

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After the customer said they have a leaking roof and want a cost to replace, the rep largely skipped diagnostic questions and went straight into intake (address/name) and scheduling without clarifying
How to fix: Before scheduling, ask 3–5 targeted discovery questions to assess urgency and scope (active leak, where it’s showing, how long, any interior damage, prior repairs/inspections). Use the answers to determine whether to offer an earlier slot or emergency guidance.
"“I’m sorry you’re dealing with a leak—are you seeing active dripping right now or water stains inside? How long has this been happening, and which area of the home is it over? Has anyone attempted a repair yet?”"
medium
Qualifying was limited to confirming the caller is the homeowner; the rep did not ask about timeline/urgency for replacement, whether they are getting other quotes, or any budget/financing considerati
How to fix: Add lightweight qualifying questions after identifying the issue and before/while scheduling: timeline, decision makers, and whether they’re comparing bids. Keep it conversational and brief.
"“When are you hoping to get this taken care of—ASAP or just planning ahead? Are you getting a couple other quotes as well? Will anyone else need to be involved in the decision?”"
high
When the customer expressed concern about wanting a certified technician and not having problems again, the rep responded with a challenging question and then reassurance that sounded dismissive/vague
How to fix: Acknowledge the concern, validate it, then provide concrete proof points (license/insurance, manufacturer certifications, what the inspection includes, photos, written scope, warranty/workmanship). Invite questions and confirm the customer’s goal.
"“I understand—no one wants to fix this twice. Our inspectors are licensed and manufacturer-certified, and during the visit they’ll take photos, identify the leak source, and walk you through options with a written proposal and warranty details. Is your main concern making sure the install is done to manufacturer specs?”"
medium
Appointment expectations were only partially set: the rep mentioned a written proposal and a 30-minute arrival notice, but did not explain what happens during the inspection, approximate duration, acc
How to fix: Add a short expectations script: duration window, what the tech will do, what the customer will receive, and confirm access/attendance requirements.
"“The inspection usually takes about 45–60 minutes. We’ll inspect the roof, attic if accessible, and take photos. Then we’ll review options and leave you a written estimate. Will you be home during the window, and is there anything we need for access—gate code, pets, or HOA rules?”"

Objections Detected (5)

medium Service Fee Concerns Unresolved
"perhaps over, but, uh, I want to estimate if a good deal, I can replay them."
💡 Acknowledge the 'good deal' concern and directly explain the estimate process and value (what the inspection covers, whether the estimate is free, what drives cost ranges), then confirm: 'Does that help?' before moving to scheduling.
medium Other Resolved
"Uh, okay, the group, there are people installed for me before, uh, 30-year, but the 50-year, they already have a leaking, so maybe when the people before you do something not correct?"
💡 Add a clearer bridge to the upcoming inspection: 'Our tech will confirm whether it’s age-related wear or an installation issue' and mention any workmanship warranty/repair standards to fully close the loop.
medium Other Unresolved
"Okay, I'd like to certify people sometime, uh, not, uh, come up to, uh, take a look at something. I'd like to certified people, uh, traditionally come to take a look and suggest, uh, what he will, uh,"
💡 Directly address verification: name the specific certifications/licenses, offer to send proof or include license numbers in the confirmation, and explain what the certified tech will do on-site; then ask for confirmation that this meets the customer's requirement before closing.
low In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"Yes, I have a licking, so I want to notice how much cost."
💡 Acknowledge the request for a price, explain that accurate pricing requires an on-site inspection due to roof size/damage/material variables, and set expectations (e.g., ballpark range if allowed, what the inspection includes, and that a written proposal will be provided after the visit). Then confirm: "Does that work for you?"
low In-Person Estimates Only Resolved
"Can somebody can stop by and take a look and see how much cost to replay the new one?"
💡 Best practice: confirm the goal of the visit (inspect leak, assess replacement vs repair options, provide written estimate) and briefly set expectations on what factors drive price so the customer feels their cost concern is being handled.

Your technician will give you a text or a phone call when he's about thirty minutes away from the property so you know exactly when to expect him.
home_ownerUh, I have a question ma'am, um, I'd like to have a…
customer_repYeah.

customer_repWhat do you mean? what do you mean?
home_ownerUh, okay, the group, there are people installed for me before, uh, 30-year, but the 50-year, they already have a leaking, so maybe when the people before you do something not correct?
customer_repUh, no, that's normal for- Uh, no, that's normal, sure. That's, that's totally normal for shingle roofs to start leaking after about fifteen to twenty years.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repYeah, and everyone here is certified. Yeah, and everyone. Um, we're licensed under the BBB, um, if you'd like to look us up at the Better Business Bureau, you can, we're all…
home_ownerMm-hmm. Okay.
home_ownerOkay, I'd like to certify people sometime, uh, not, uh, come up to, uh, take a look at something. I'd like to certified people, uh, traditionally come to take a look and suggest, uh, what he will, uh, do the best for my group.

home_ownerOkay.

Summary

The homeowner called Arizona Roofers to ask about certified shingle roof installation and to get pricing because their shingle roof is leaking and they are considering replacing it. The representative confirmed the roof type, collected the property address, name, phone number, permission to text, and email for sending a written proposal. The homeowner stated the roof was originally a 30-year shingle roof and is now around 20 years old, and confirmed there are no solar panels (but there is an air conditioning unit on the roof). The rep explained that leaks after about 15–20 years can be normal for shingle roofs and reassured the customer that the company is certified and established, then scheduled an on-site appointment for Thursday from 1–3 PM with confirmation and pre-arrival communication.

• Customer has a shingle roof that is leaking and wants an estimate for replacing it with a new roof.
• Roof age shared by customer: originally a 30-year roof, currently around 20 years old; customer stated there are no solar panels and mentioned an air conditioning unit on the roof.
• Appointment was booked for Thursday from 1–3 PM; customer agreed to receive text updates and provided an email for the written proposal.

Next Steps

➤ Technician to visit the property during the scheduled Thursday 1–3 PM window to assess the leaking shingle roof and provide recommendations.
➤ Send the written proposal/estimate to the customer via the provided email after the visit.

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After the customer stated they have a leaking roof and want an estimate, the rep quickly switched to intake (address/name) and scheduling without clarifying the problem details (how long leaking, acti
How to fix: Before scheduling, ask 3–5 targeted discovery questions to assess urgency and scope, then route appropriately (emergency vs standard inspection).
"“I can absolutely get someone out. Before I book it, is the leak active right now? How long has it been happening, and have you noticed any water stains or damage inside? Do you know which area of the roof it’s coming from, and has anyone looked at it yet?”"
medium
Qualifying was incomplete: the rep confirmed the caller is the homeowner but did not ask about decision-makers being present, urgency/timeline for replacement, whether they are comparing quotes, or an
How to fix: Add lightweight BANT questions after confirming homeowner status and before finalizing the appointment.
"“Great—will you be the one making the decision, or does anyone else need to be there for the estimate? How soon are you hoping to get this addressed? Are you getting other quotes as well? And would you like to review financing options if replacement makes sense?”"
medium
Expectation-setting focused only on texts/arrival timing; the rep did not explain what happens during the inspection, how long it takes, what the customer will receive (repair vs replacement options),
How to fix: Briefly outline the appointment flow, duration, and deliverables, and confirm access requirements.
"“The inspection usually takes about 45–60 minutes. The technician will get on the roof, take photos, check the leak area, and then walk you through options—repair vs replacement—with a written estimate. Is there anything we need for access like a gate code or pets in the yard?”"
high
When the customer raised a quality/certification concern, the rep responded defensively and vaguely (“What do you mean?” and “no one's gonna steer you wrong”) and gave an inaccurate-sounding credibili
How to fix: Acknowledge the concern, reassure with specific credentials/process, and confirm the customer feels comfortable proceeding.
"“I understand—nobody wants to fix this twice. Our technicians are licensed and insured, and we follow manufacturer installation standards. At the visit, we’ll identify the root cause of the leak and show you photos so you can see exactly what we’re recommending. Does that address your concern about having a certified professional out?”"

Objections Detected (5)

low In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"perhaps over, but, uh, I want to estimate if a good deal, I can replay them."
💡 Acknowledge the desire for a 'good deal' and explain how the estimate works (inspection is needed to price accurately), what the proposal will include (scope, materials, warranty), and set expectations on any fees (if applicable) before scheduling.
medium Other Resolved
"Uh, okay, the group, there are people installed for me before, uh, 30-year, but the 50-year, they already have a leaking, so maybe when the people before you do something not correct?"
💡 Add specifics: explain common leak causes at that age and what the inspection will verify (flashing, underlayment, penetrations), plus any workmanship warranty offered on replacement.
low In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"Yes, I have a licking, so I want to notice how much cost."
💡 Acknowledge the request for a price, explain that an accurate estimate requires an on-site inspection (roof size, layers, damage extent, materials), and set expectations (e.g., free inspection, written proposal after visit, and optionally a rough range if appropriate).
low In-Person Estimates Only Resolved
"Can somebody can stop by and take a look and see how much cost to replay the new one?"
💡 Best practice: confirm it’s a free/no-obligation inspection (if true) and clarify that the written estimate will be provided after the technician evaluates the roof.
medium Other Unresolved
"Uh, before you do the work, um, I don't know if you do something wrong or what, so I need to certify the technician to install, to take a look and see what he suggests. I don't want to, um, have probl"
💡 Directly address trust/qualification: specify what certifications/licenses the technician holds, offer to send proof (license number, certifications, BBB link), explain the inspection process and quality controls/warranty, and confirm: "Would that address your concern about having problems again?"

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Roofers. This is Bronte. How can I help you?
home_ownerOh, hi. I checked on the website and I see your guy have a certified, uh, roping installation for, um, um, for single to roping.

customer_repOkay, what's your property address?

customer_repOkay, give me just one second.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repOkay, and what is your first and last name?
home_ownerMy foot name, C-H-I-N-H, not name form, not name form P-S-A-M.
customer_repOkay. And are you the homeowner?
home_owneryeah.
customer_repOkay, is this phone number the best way to reach you?
home_owneryeah.

home_ownerSure.
customer_repOkay. And then what's the best email for you? That's how we'll send over the written proposal.

customer_repOkay. And how old is the roof?
home_ownerUh, the roof, uh, you install, uh, the roof a 30-year, but, uh, just, uh, right now only around 20-year, but uh, around acquisition, I think you have licking, I already have a…
customer_repOkay.
home_ownerperhaps over, but, uh, I want to estimate if a good deal, I can replay them.
customer_repOkay. And, um, is the property in an HOA community?
home_ownerUh, as so I know, okay.
customer_repAnd do you have any solar panels on the roof?
home_ownerUh, no.
customer_repOkay.
home_ownerI have, uh, air conditioning on the roof.
customer_repOkay. All right, I can have somebody out to you, uh, this Thursday, February 12th at 1 p.m., is that? Is that? work for you?
home_ownerI'll go back. Oh, what's that?
customer_repOkay, I said, uh, uh, Thursday, uh, okay, um, you said one o'clock? One to three PM, does that work for you?
home_ownerOh, okay, yeah, one, two, three, five. Oh.
customer_repokay. I'll book that for you and then once we're off this phone call, you'll get a confirmation text with the appointment details and then the day of your appointment. one. Your technician will give you a text or a phone call when he's about thirty minutes away from the property so you know exactly when to expect him.
home_ownerUh, I have a question ma'am, um, I'd like to have a…
customer_repYeah.
home_ownerUh, before you do the work, um, I don't know if you do something wrong or what, so I need to certify the technician to install, to take a look and see what he suggests. I don't want to, um, have problem again.
customer_repWhat do you mean? what do you mean?
home_ownerUh, okay, the group, there are people installed for me before, uh, 30-year, but the 50-year, they already have a leaking, so maybe when the people before you do something not correct?
customer_repUh, no, that's normal for- Uh, no, that's normal, sure. That's, that's totally normal for shingle roofs to start leaking after about fifteen to twenty years.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repYeah, and everyone here is certified. Yeah, and everyone. Um, we're licensed under the BBB, um, if you'd like to look us up at the Better Business Bureau, you can, we're all…
home_ownerMm-hmm. Okay.
home_ownerOkay, I'd like to certify people sometime, uh, not, uh, come up to, uh, take a look at something. I'd like to certified people, uh, traditionally come to take a look and suggest, uh, what he will, uh, do the best for my group.
customer_repYeah, we've, we've been in business for about twenty-eight years, so no one's gonna steer you wrong.
home_ownerOkay.
customer_repAll right. So we'll be on Thursday from one to three. Yep, and they'll give you a text before they arrive.
home_ownerSure, thank you very much, Matt.
customer_repOf course, have a good day too.
home_ownerAlright, thank you very much. Bye bye.
customer_repbye bye.
Call #3alshiana+14805551234completedNew InquiryWARM

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_05
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments15

Summary

Alshiana, a real estate agent in Arizona, called on behalf of a client who is under contract to purchase a property with a roof in rough condition and requested a quote to understand the cost to fix it as soon as possible. Steve explained that for real estate transactions the company charges a one-time $399 fee for a technician to come out and provide a full estimate and report with approximately 15–100 photos, and that the fee can be applied toward repairs or replacement if they choose to proceed with the company. Alshiana said she needs to confirm with her clients whether they are okay with the fee since they would be responsible for paying it, and she will call back; no appointment was scheduled during the call.

• Caller is a real estate agent (Alshiana) calling on behalf of a client purchasing a property; the roof is described as in rough condition and they want a quote to see how much it would be to fix.
• The representative stated that for real estate transactions there is a one-time $399 fee for a technician to provide a full estimate/report with about 15–100 photos, and the fee goes toward repairs and/or replacement if they choose the company.
• Caller did not schedule; she will check with her clients about the $399 fee (since they would pay) and get back to the company.

Action Items

☑ Wait for the caller to confirm with her clients regarding approval of the $399 real estate inspection/estimate fee.
☑ Be prepared to schedule a technician visit for a full estimate/report once the client approves the fee.
☑ Follow up with the customer to get the decision after they check with their clients about approving the estimate fee

Next Steps

➤ Caller to contact her clients and decide whether to proceed with the $399 fee.
➤ Caller to call back to schedule if the clients approve.
Sentiment
65%
Summary Confidence
90%

SOP Compliance

42%
Followed (2/7)
Missed (6/7)
Greeting & IdentificationQualifying (timeline)Needs DiscoveryQualifying (decision maker/authority + contact verification)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsObjection HandlingClose & Confirmation

AI Coaching Recommendations (5)

medium
After the caller explained the roof is in rough condition and they want a quote, the rep moved quickly to the $399 real-estate transaction fee without gathering basic job details (roof type, size, age
How to fix: Before discussing fees, ask 4–6 targeted discovery questions to understand scope and urgency, then connect the fee to the customer’s goal (buyer due diligence) and the specific deliverables they need for the transaction.
"“To make sure we get you the right quote for your buyer, can I ask a few quick questions—what type of roof is it, about how old, is there active leaking or visible damage, and what’s the property address? Also, do you need the report by a specific inspection deadline?”"
high
The rep did not verify who the decision maker is (agent vs. buyer) or capture/confirm contact details (phone/email) for follow-up when the caller said they needed to check with their clients.
How to fix: Confirm authority and collect contact info early, then set a specific follow-up plan when the caller needs approval from someone else.
"“Totally understand—will your client be the one approving and paying the $399? What’s the best phone number and email for them (or for you) so I can send the fee details and sample report, and when should I call you back—today at 3 or tomorrow at 10?”"
high
When the caller hesitated on the fee, the rep did not handle the objection (no empathy + value reinforcement + alternative options) and allowed the call to end without offering to tentatively schedule
How to fix: Use an objection-handling sequence: acknowledge, clarify, reinforce value, and offer options (send details, speak to buyer, schedule a tentative slot, or propose a shorter consult call).
"“I understand—$399 is an investment. The reason is the technician provides a full photo report and written estimate you can use in negotiations. If it helps, I can email you a sample report and the fee policy right now, and we can hold a tentative appointment for tomorrow—if your client says no, we’ll cancel. Would that work?”"
high
The rep did not attempt scheduling (no dates/times offered) despite saying they could check the schedule, and did not confirm address/access details required for an inspection.
How to fix: Offer two concrete appointment windows and confirm property address and access requirements (lockbox, gate code, HOA, occupant).
"“I can get someone out quickly—would today 2–4 or tomorrow 9–11 work? What’s the property address, and is there a lockbox or someone who needs to be present?”"
medium
The close lacked confirmation and next steps: no recap, no promise to send information, and no agreed follow-up time after the caller said they would get back to the company.
How to fix: Summarize, confirm next step, and set a specific follow-up time; offer to send a text/email with fee details and what the inspection includes.
"“No problem—I'll send you a quick email with the $399 inspection details and what’s included. When is the best time to follow up after you speak with your client—later today or tomorrow morning?”"

Objections Detected (1)

medium Service Fee Concerns Unresolved
"Um, I'll have to check up my clients to see if they're okay with the $300 fee, cause it'll be them that had to take care of it."
💡 Reduce friction by proposing a concrete next step: offer to schedule a tentative inspection time pending client approval, offer to email a written explanation of the fee/value for the client, or offer to speak directly with the client to answer questions; then confirm a follow-up time.

API returned 1 pending actions → 1 passed revenue-impact filter

Transcript (15 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona roopers. My name is Steve. How can I help you?
home_ownerhi, this is alshiana. i'm a real estate agent out here in arizona. i have a client that, um, is looking to purchase a property and the roof is pretty in pretty rough condition, but we would like to get a quote on it just to make sure, um, just to see how much it would be to fix it. is that something that you guys could do?
customer_repYeah, of course. Okay. We do. Um, are you guys in the contract stage or?
home_ownerYes.
customer_repOkay, perfect. Um, so I'm assuming you have a timeline when you need to have that.
home_ownerYes. So it's as soon as you guys would be able to do it. So just as soon as you guys would be able to do it.
customer_repGotcha. I could take a look at my schedule here. Um, so just a heads up for real estate transactions, we do charge a one time fee of $3.99 to have a technician come out and give you a full estimate full report. They take about fifteen to a hundred photos, write up a report.
home_ownerOkay. like the whole thing um.
customer_repMm-hmm. So if you're interested. So if you're interested. It was $399 and then that fee goes towards…
home_ownerI know that. Three ninety-nine, three hundred ninety-nine, um,
customer_repit goes towards any repairs and or replacements if you guys choose to go with us as well. So if you're interested, we can go ahead and… Okay. get that scheduled for you?
home_ownerUm, I'll have to check up my clients to see if they're okay with the $300 fee, cause it'll be them that had to take care of it.
customer_repOkay, yeah, don't worry.
home_ownerYeah, I'll get back to you guys. Thank you for my job.
customer_repYeah, have a good day.

Segments11

Summary

Alshiana, a real estate agent in Arizona, called on behalf of a client who is under contract to purchase a property with a roof in rough condition and requested a quote to understand the cost to fix it. Steve confirmed they can provide an estimate and asked about the transaction stage and timeline; Alshiana said they need it as soon as possible. Steve explained that for real estate transactions there is a one-time $399 fee for a technician to come out, take 15–100 photos, and produce a full estimate and report, and that the fee would be applied toward any repairs or replacement if they choose to use the company. Alshiana said she needs to check with her clients about the fee since they would be responsible for paying it, and the call ended without scheduling an appointment.

• Caller is a real estate agent (Alshiana) calling for a client under contract to purchase a property with a roof in rough condition and wants a quote to fix it.
• Customer requested the inspection/estimate as soon as the company can do it.
• Rep stated real estate transactions require a one-time $399 fee for a technician to provide a full estimate/report with 15–100 photos, and the fee applies toward repairs/replacement if they proceed; caller will check with clients and no appointment was scheduled.

Action Items

☑ Call the customer/agent back after they confirm with their clients about the $300 fee to proceed with scheduling the roof estimate/inspection

Next Steps

➤ Caller to confirm with her clients whether they approve the $399 fee.
➤ If approved, caller to contact the company back to schedule the technician estimate/report.

AI Coaching Recommendations (5)

medium
After the caller requested a quote for a roof in rough condition, the rep did not ask any diagnostic questions (roof type/age, extent of issues, leaks, prior inspections) and moved quickly to discussi
How to fix: Before quoting fees, ask 4–6 targeted discovery questions to understand condition, urgency, and property details, then connect those answers to why the inspection/report is valuable.
"“To make sure we give your client an accurate quote, can I ask a few quick questions—are there any active leaks, do you know the roof type (tile/shingle/flat), approximate age, and is it a one- or two-story home? Has anyone inspected it yet?”"
high
The rep did not collect or verify essential information (property address, best callback number/email, client/decision maker details) before ending the call.
How to fix: Capture contact and property details early (agent + homeowner if possible) and confirm the best method/time to follow up.
"“What’s the property address for the estimate, and what’s the best phone number and email to send the fee details and report info? Also, is your client the homeowner/decision maker, and should they be included on the confirmation?”"
high
When the caller objected that they needed to check with their clients about the $399 fee, the rep did not acknowledge, clarify, or offer options; instead, the rep ended the call abruptly.
How to fix: Acknowledge the concern, reinforce value, and set a concrete follow-up plan (time/date) or offer to speak with the client directly; optionally offer to email a written explanation.
"“Totally understand. Would it help if I emailed a breakdown of what’s included and that the $399 is credited toward repairs? When can I follow up—today at 3 or tomorrow morning? If your client prefers, I can also hop on a quick call with them.”"
high
Scheduling was not attempted even though the caller indicated urgency (“as soon as you guys would be able to do it”) and the rep said they could look at the schedule.
How to fix: Offer 2–3 specific inspection windows and explain what’s needed for access; if fee approval is pending, offer a tentative hold pending confirmation.
"“I can get you on the calendar—does tomorrow 10–12 or Thursday 1–3 work? If you’re waiting on approval, I can tentatively hold a slot and you can confirm once your client signs off.”"
medium
Close & confirmation steps were missing: no recap of next steps, no commitment for follow-up, and no confirmation method (text/email).
How to fix: Summarize the plan, confirm who will decide, and lock a follow-up time plus delivery method for fee/inspection details.
"“Just to recap: the inspection/report is $399 credited toward repairs. I’ll email that summary now, and I’ll call you tomorrow at 10am to see if your client wants to move forward—does that work?”"

Objections Detected (2)

medium Service Fee Concerns Unresolved
"Okay. get that scheduled for you? Um, I'll have to check up my clients to see if they're okay with the $300 fee, cause it'll be them that had to take care of it."
💡 Acknowledge the concern, then reframe value by clearly explaining what the $399 includes (photos, written report, technician time) and that it’s credited toward repairs/replacement. Then offer a concrete next step: ask when they can confirm with the client and schedule a follow-up call, or propose a tentative appointment pending client approval (if allowed), and confirm who will pay and how.
low In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"we would like to get a quote on it just to make sure, um, just to see how much it would be to fix it."
💡 Acknowledge the request for a quick quote, explain why an accurate price can’t be given over the phone without an inspection, then offer a helpful range based on roof type/size if possible and clearly justify the $399 inspection/report value (photos, written report, credit toward work). Close with a clear next step: offer to tentatively schedule while they confirm with the client or offer to email the fee details/report sample for approval.

Transcript (11 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona roopers. My name is Steve. How can I help you?
home_ownerhi, this is alshiana. i'm a real estate agent out here in arizona. i have a client that, um, is looking to purchase a property and the roof is pretty in pretty rough condition, but we would like to get a quote on it just to make sure, um, just to see how much it would be to fix it. is that something that you guys could do?
customer_repYeah, of course. Okay. We do. Um, are you guys in the contract stage or?
home_ownerYes.
customer_repOkay, perfect. Um, so I'm assuming you have a timeline when you need to have that.
home_ownerYes. So it's as soon as you guys would be able to do it. So just as soon as you guys would be able to do it.
customer_repGotcha. I could take a look at my schedule here. Um, so just a heads up for real estate transactions, we do charge a one time fee of $3.99 to have a technician come out and give you a full estimate full report. They take about fifteen to a hundred photos, write up a report.
home_ownerOkay. like the whole thing um.
customer_repMm-hmm. So if you're interested. So if you're interested. It was $399 and then that fee goes towards… I know that. Three ninety-nine, three hundred ninety-nine, um, it goes towards any repairs and or replacements if you guys choose to go with us as well. So if you're interested, we can go ahead and…
home_ownerOkay. get that scheduled for you? Um, I'll have to check up my clients to see if they're okay with the $300 fee, cause it'll be them that had to take care of it.
customer_repOkay, yeah, don't worry. Yeah, I'll get back to you guys. Thank you for my job. Yeah, have a good day.

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_08
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments50

Summary

Jennifer Barker from Optimum/Optical Medical called Arizona Roofers to request a roof inspection for a commercial property they own at 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ, which is being converted from a former bank into a primary care clinic. She reported that contractors recently found a couple of roof leaks over the last few days and asked if someone could inspect as soon as tomorrow (2026-03-03). Steve explained he could not guarantee a next-day visit but took down the property and contact details and said a technician would reach out to coordinate a mutually workable date/time. Jennifer confirmed the building is single-story with a flat roof, no known gate code requirements, and that contractors should be on site during the inspection; she preferred text message updates and agreed proposals/reports could be sent via email.

• Customer reported a couple of roof leaks at a commercial property in Surprise and requested an inspection as soon as 2026-03-03.
• Property details provided: 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ 85374; commercial building (former bank being converted to a primary care clinic), single-story, flat roof; roof age unknown; no known gate codes.
• Outcome: No inspection was scheduled on the call; the rep collected contact info and stated a technician will contact Jennifer to coordinate the inspection time/date; customer prefers text updates and will receive proposals via email.

Action Items

☑ Technician to contact Jennifer Barker at 909-684-6098 to schedule the roof inspection for the Surprise property.
☑ Send inspection report/proposal to Jennifer via email (jbarker@optimaZ.com as spelled on the call) and provide text message updates as requested.

Next Steps

➤ Confirm a date/time for the on-site roof inspection and ensure someone is present on site (contractors or authorized contact) during the visit.
➤ Perform the roof inspection and deliver the photo documentation and written report with options to the customer.
Sentiment
78%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

78%
Followed (4/7)
Missed (3/7)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT partial)Setting Expectations (partial)Scheduling (no appointment set/confirmed)Close & Confirmation (no confirmation message or recap)Objection Handling (not applicable—no objections raised)

AI Coaching Recommendations (5)

high
After the caller requested an inspection 'tomorrow,' the rep said they couldn't guarantee it and only offered that a technician would 'coordinate a time and date' instead of proposing and confirming a
How to fix: Offer the next available time windows and attempt to book on the call. If dispatch must confirm, set a concrete commitment (e.g., technician will call within X minutes/hours) and provide a provisional window for the visit.
"“I can’t promise tomorrow yet, but I can offer two windows: 8–11 AM or 12–3 PM. Which works best? If those fill up, my technician will call you within 30 minutes to confirm the exact time.”"
medium
Once the customer reported roof leaks, the rep did not ask key diagnostic/urgency questions (when it started, whether water is actively entering, interior damage, number/location of leaks) before movi
How to fix: Add a short urgency triage before scheduling: when it started, active leaking now, any ceiling/wall damage, and where the leaks are occurring.
"“When did you first notice the leaks, and is water actively coming in right now? Any interior damage to ceilings or walls, and do you know roughly where on the roof it’s showing up?”"
medium
Decision-maker/on-site authorization was left unclear: the rep noted they require someone on-site, but when the caller said only contractors would be there and no owners/managers, the rep did not reso
How to fix: Confirm who will be on-site, their name/role, and whether they can grant access. If not, arrange for an authorized decision-maker to be present or available by phone during the inspection.
"“Who specifically will be on-site (name and number), and can they provide roof access? If not, can you or another authorized decision-maker be available by phone during the inspection so we can review findings and next steps?”"
low
The opening clarification about the email was abrupt and confusing, creating a less professional first impression before rapport was established.
How to fix: Acknowledge the email, confirm the address calmly, and transition back to the customer’s need.
"“Thanks for letting me know you emailed us. Just to confirm, was it to info@arizonaroofers.com? Great—tell me a bit about the leaks and what you’re hoping to accomplish tomorrow.”"
medium
The rep did not close with a clear recap and confirmation of next steps (who will contact, by when, and via what channel), nor did they confirm that a text/email confirmation would be sent.
How to fix: Summarize the key details captured and set a specific follow-up SLA (e.g., technician will call within X hours) and confirm the preferred contact method.
"“To recap: inspection at 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise. Best number is 909-684-6098 and we’ll text updates. Our technician will call you within the next hour to confirm the appointment time. If you don’t hear from us, you can call back at this number.”"

Transcript (50 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Roofers. My name is Steve. How can I help you out?
home_ownerMy name is Jennifer Barker. I'm with Optical Medical, and I sent an email a little bit earlier today. I have a site enterprise that
customer_repWhat? Oh. You send an email to… You think- What, you sent the email to what email, sorry? The email to what email first?
home_ownerThe info at Arizona Roofers.
customer_repOkay.
home_ownerI have a location in surprise that we are currently in process of making into a clinic, and over the last couple of days, they found some roof leaks, and I'm looking to see if you guys have somebody available tomorrow that can do an inspection for me.
customer_repDo you know how old the roof is?
home_ownerI am. no idea. And that's a… Unfortunately, I have very, I have very little information about the, about the, um, the age of the roof or anything like that.
customer_repOkay, and this is a commercial property.
home_ownerIt is.
customer_repOkay, I'm not sure if I can guarantee someone coming out there tomorrow, um, but we could take your information down and I can give it to my technician and he can coordinate a time and date, um, MS, do you own the property or are you a project?
home_ownerWe do own it. We do own it, yes.
customer_repGotcha. And what type of a clinic is it?
home_ownerWell, it's a primary care medical clinic.
customer_repOkay.
home_ownerBut it was, it was a bank and we're converting it into a clinic.
customer_repGotcha, and you're just experiencing that, those couple of leaks on-
home_ownerYes.
customer_repOkay, let me grab your information down and then I will have a technician reach out to you. Um, can I start with the property address?
home_ownerSure, it's one, three, nine, two, six, West Bell Road, in surprise, and it's eight, five, three, seven, four.
customer_repNo. You're gonna be the point of-
home_ownerI am the main contact, yes. I will not be on Facebook.
customer_repOkay, can I get yours? Okay, is someone gonna be on site? We do require someone to be on site during time of inspection, preferably.
home_ownerI believe I believe we do have, um, our contractors there. You have, um, our contractors there.
customer_repOkay, you don't have any decision makers that aren't gonna be able to be on site, like any owners or managers or anything like that?
home_ownerNo, not that I'm aware of, no.
customer_repOkay. Okay. Can I grab your first and last name?
home_ownerSure. It's Jennifer Barker, and it's B. S. A. R. K. E. R. .
customer_repAnd Jennifer, what's your role there? Are you, uh, just… Okay. And so… Thank you. here. Okay, perfect. Can I grab your email, Jennifer?
home_ownerSure, it's J. Barker, again, B. S. P. L. A. R. K. E. R., at optimal. After… That's O. P. is in Paul. T. is in Tom. I. M. A. Mm-hmm. A is in apple, Z is in zebra dot com.
customer_repOkay. Then the- The last phone number to reach you?
home_ownerUh, my cell phone is nine zero nine six eight four six zero nine eight.
customer_repOkay. Am I able to send you email and text message updates about the roof?
home_ownerText message update. Yeah.
customer_repOkay. Do you know what type of roofing material is on the roof?
home_ownerTile should color flat. Uh, it's a flat roof. But okay.
customer_repIs this a single story, uh, building?
home_ownerYes. Mm-hmm.
customer_repDo you know how big it is, square feet-wise?
home_ownerI don't have information straight off the end, but I can probably figure it out, but I don't have it with me.
customer_repOkay, no worries. Um, is there any gate codes we should be aware of?
home_ownerand not that i'm aware of, no.
customer_repOkay, I'm assuming there's no HOA since it's commercial, and who do, who owns the property?
home_ownerUh, Optimum does.
customer_repAnd then I'm assuming we're gonna be sending, so we'll have the technician coordinate with you and then still be sending all that information to your email, like proposals and stuff.
home_ownerMm-hmm.
customer_repOkay. All right, I'll go ahead and let my technician know, and he'll reach out with you with the time and day that works for both of you. Um, how it kind of works is when they come out there, you'll be receiving, uh, when they come out here, they do a full roof inspection, they take about fifty to a hundred photos, we write a full report, um, with all of your options, and then I know if you guys choose to go with the same day, they do take some money off the top as well. But if you don't have any other questions, Jennifer, I, you'll be getting reached out to,
home_ownerNow, I appreciate that. Thank you so much.
customer_repOf course, no problem. Have a good day.
home_ownerYou too.

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_09
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments7

Summary

The caller, who is in the process of buying a home currently in escrow, said a full home inspection was completed on 2026-03-02 and they now need a roof inspection as soon as possible. The representative confirmed they likely have availability to get someone out ASAP and explained that for real estate transactions there is a one-time $3.99 fee that would be applied toward any needed repairs or replacement. The caller stated they are calling around for pricing, declined to schedule at that moment, and said they would call back after making a few more calls; the call ended without an appointment being booked.

• Caller is buying a home in escrow and requested a roof inspection after a full inspection on 2026-03-02 indicated one was needed.
• Caller asked for someone to come out ASAP (interpretable as by 2026-03-03 per instructions).
• Rep stated real estate transaction roof inspections have a one-time $3.99 fee that goes toward repairs and/or replacements; caller chose to shop prices and call back, so no appointment was scheduled.

Action Items

☑ Be prepared to schedule a roof inspection quickly if the caller phones back after comparing prices.
☑ If the caller returns, confirm property address and any access details needed for the inspection (not provided on this call).

Next Steps

➤ Customer will make additional pricing calls and then call back if they decide to proceed.
➤ Company to schedule the roof inspection upon customer callback and acceptance of the $3.99 real estate transaction fee.
Sentiment
62%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

42%
Followed (1/7)
Missed (6/7)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)Scheduling (if applicable)Setting ExpectationsObjection HandlingClose & Confirmation

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After the caller explained they are in escrow and need a roof inspection ASAP, the rep immediately introduced a $3.99 real-estate transaction fee and pivoted to scheduling without asking any diagnosti
How to fix: Acknowledge urgency, then ask 3–5 targeted discovery questions before discussing fees: escrow deadline, inspection purpose (certification vs. condition report), property type/stories, roof material/age (if known), and whether any active leaks were noted. Then connect the fee to the deliverable and timeline.
"“Absolutely—we help with escrow roof inspections. What’s your deadline to submit the roof report? Did the home inspector note any active leaks or specific concerns? Do you know the roof type (tile/shingle/flat) and how many stories the home is? Once I have that, I can confirm the soonest appointment and what you’ll receive.”"
high
When the caller said they were calling around for prices and would call back, the rep accepted the brush-off and ended the call without addressing the objection, offering value, or attempting to secur
How to fix: Use a brief objection-handling sequence: acknowledge, clarify what they’re comparing, reinforce differentiators (speed for escrow, deliverable/report, licensed/insured), and offer a low-commitment next step (hold a slot, refundable/credited fee, or scheduled callback). Always ask for a specific follow-up time and capture contact details.
"“Totally understand—most buyers compare a couple options. Just so I can give you an apples-to-apples comparison, are you mainly comparing the inspection fee or the turnaround time for the report? We can typically get out there ASAP for escrow and provide a written roof condition report. I can hold the earliest slot for you—would tomorrow morning or this afternoon work better? If you’d rather decide after your calls, what’s the best number and a good time to follow up in 30 minutes?”"
medium
The rep did not collect the caller’s name, phone number, property address, or email at any point, so there was no way to send details, confirm availability, or follow up after the caller deferred.
How to fix: Early in the call, capture at minimum: name, callback number, property address, and email/text permission for confirmation. If the caller won’t book, still ask to send info and set a follow-up time.
"“Before you make your other calls, can I grab your name and the property address? I can text/email our inspection details and pricing so you can compare easily. What’s the best number and email for you?”"
medium
The rep did not set expectations for what the roof inspection includes, how long it takes, or what the customer receives (e.g., written report/photos) which is especially important for escrow transact
How to fix: Briefly outline the appointment flow and deliverables before asking to schedule: duration, inspection scope, report format, and turnaround time for documentation.
"“The inspection usually takes about 30–45 minutes, and we’ll provide a written roof condition summary with photos the same day/within 24 hours—perfect for escrow. Then we’ll review any repair or replacement options if needed.”"

Objections Detected (1)

medium Service Fee Concerns Unresolved
"um okay. let me um so i'm calling around for prices um and let let me go ahead and just make a couple more calls and then um i can give you a call back if everything works out."
💡 Acknowledge the price-shopping, then directly address the fee/value and create a next step: briefly explain what the $3.99 covers (e.g., priority scheduling/inspection coordination/credit toward repairs), confirm total expected inspection cost and turnaround time, and offer to tentatively book the earliest slot (with an easy cancel option) to meet escrow deadlines.

Transcript (7 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona referee, my name is Deva, how can I help you?
home_ownerhi, i am calling because i am in the process of um buying a home, it's currently in escrow and we just had a full inspection today and it looks like we're gonna need a roof inspection so i'm trying to see. And we just had a full inspection today, and it looks like we're gonna need a roof inspection, so I'm trying to… i can find anybody that could get out there like asap and help us out with that.
customer_repYeah, yeah, uh, I'm sure we can, I'm pretty sure that we have availability to get out there ASAP, um, for any real estate transactions we do charge a one time fee of $3.99 and that would go towards any repairs and or replacements depending on what you need, so if you're interested I can go ahead and. For any real estate transactions, we do charge a one time fee of $3.99 and that would go towards any repairs and or replacements depending on what you need. So if you're interested, I can go ahead and schedule that.
home_ownerum okay. let me um so i'm calling around for prices um and let let me go ahead and just make a couple more calls and then um i can give you a call back if everything works out.
customer_repokay. Okay, sounds good, no worries.
home_ownerAll right, thank you.
customer_repOf course, have a great day.
Call #7Joey Hallis+14805551234completedConfirmationWARM

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_07
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments18

Summary

Joey Hallis called Arizona Rufus asking to speak with Travis Jones regarding a roof inspection appointment on Sierra, stating the roof tech had not scheduled it but was now on the way. The representative confirmed the appointment on the calendar, transferred the call to Travis, and Travis confirmed the technician’s arrival window of 4:30–5:00 (possibly earlier). Joey agreed to wait at the property and requested that the technician leave a voicemail because unknown numbers are routed to voicemail on his phone. Travis said he would have the technician reach out, and the call ended with confirmation of the plan.

• Caller Joey Hallis asked to speak with Travis Jones about a roof tech coming out for an inspection on Sierra and said the tech previously "dropped the ball" and didn’t get it scheduled.
• Travis confirmed the technician is on the way and expected to arrive between 4:30 and 5:00 (possibly a little sooner), and the customer agreed to wait.
• Customer noted calls from non-contacts go to voicemail and asked that the technician leave a voice message when calling.

Action Items

☑ Notify the technician to call the customer and leave a voicemail if the call is not answered due to unknown-number screening.
☑ Confirm internally that the inspection appointment for Sierra is active on the schedule and aligned with the 4:30–5:00 arrival window.

Next Steps

➤ Technician arrives at the property between 4:30 and 5:00 on 2026-03-02 (or slightly earlier) to perform the roof inspection.
➤ Technician contacts the customer en route and leaves a voicemail if the call goes to voicemail.
Sentiment
62%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

62%
Followed (2/4)
Missed (2/4)
Greeting & IdentificationConfirm Appointment DetailsAnswer Questions (if any)Close Professionally

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After the homeowner asked to speak to Travis Jones, the rep said they would transfer and then stated, "i have travis in the line for you now," but the rep continued speaking and the caller later said,
How to fix: Execute a clean warm transfer: confirm the transfer, introduce the caller to Travis, then drop off the line. If Travis is unavailable, clearly state that and offer to take a message or provide a callback time.
"“Joey, I’m going to connect you with Travis now. One moment. Travis, I have Joey Hallis on the line calling about today’s roof inspection arrival window. Joey, you’re connected with Travis—I'll let you two talk.”"
medium
The rep confirmed only a broad arrival window and did not verify the appointment basics (service address, date/today confirmation, tech name, best callback number) before ending the call.
How to fix: Use a quick confirmation checklist: confirm it’s for today, confirm the service address, confirm the assigned tech name, and confirm the best phone number for day-of contact (and whether texting is preferred).
"“Just to confirm, this is for today at [address]. The technician is [name], arriving between 4:30–5:00. What’s the best number for him to call or text when he’s on the way?”"
medium
When the homeowner raised a contactability issue (unknown numbers go to voicemail), the rep did not provide a solution (e.g., confirm the calling number, offer a text, or provide the tech’s number) an
How to fix: Acknowledge and offer a concrete workaround: confirm the number the tech will call from (if available), ask permission to text from the office, or instruct the customer to temporarily disable call screening for the next hour. Confirm the plan back.
"“Thanks for the heads-up. I’ll have the tech text you first from our office number so you can save it, and if he calls from a different number he’ll leave a voicemail. Does that work?”"
low
The call ended without a clear professional close or final confirmation of next steps; the rep trailed off with "and, uh. Yeah." and did not restate the plan or thank the customer.
How to fix: Close with a concise recap and a courteous sign-off: arrival window, who will contact them, and what to do if they miss the call. Offer a callback number if needed.
"“Perfect—thanks for waiting. The tech will arrive between 4:30–5:00 and will call/text when he’s close; if you miss it, he’ll leave a voicemail. If anything changes, you can call us back at this number. Thank you, Joey.”"

Objections Detected (2)

medium Inefficient Agent Communication Resolved
"In regards to the tech, the roof tech coming out to do the inspection on Sierra, he dropped the ball and didn't get it scheduled, and he wanted to know if they can, he's on his way, so he'll be here s"
💡 Best practice: explicitly apologize for the scheduling miss, confirm the exact appointment window and technician name, and restate the plan (tech en route + will call/text on arrival) to reassure the customer the issue is fully corrected.
low Other Unresolved
"I will, I'm not sure what number he's going to call on because it's, if I don't, I have my phone set up that if you're not a contact, it automatically goes to voicemail."
💡 Best practice: confirm and operationalize the workaround (e.g., "Got it—I’ll note to leave a voicemail and also text you from our main number"), verify the best callback number, and repeat back the instruction to ensure the tech follows it.

Transcript (18 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Rufus. This is Matty. How can I help you?
home_ownerHello, Matty. Can I speak to Travis Jones again? This is Joey Hallis.
customer_repyeah, in regards to what?
home_ownerIn regards to the tech, the roof tech coming out to do the inspection on Sierra, he dropped the ball and didn't get it scheduled, and he wanted to know if they can, he's on his way, so he'll be here sometime between 4.30 and 5.00.
customer_repyeah yeah i do see that here on the calendar let me go ahead and get you over to travis.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repalright julia i have travis in the line for you now.
home_ownerHello, Travis.
customer_repAlright. All right. so. We can hang out here. Can you guys still make it sometime between 4.30 and 5?00? he is on his way, he might be a little bit sooner than that, would that be okay?
home_ownerOkay, that's fine. We'll stay here and wait for them.
customer_repi appreciate it and i'll have them reach out to you okay?
home_owneralright.
customer_repand i'll have them reach out to you, okay?
home_ownerokay, appreciate it, yeah.
customer_repand, uh. Yeah.
home_ownerI will, I'm not sure what number he's going to call on because it's, if I don't, I have my phone set up that if you're not a contact, it automatically goes to voicemail.
customer_repOh, that's fair. It might not work then.
home_ownerOh, that's fair. It might not work then. Tell him to leave a, tell him to leave a voice message. I do. Yeah. Ah. Thank you so much, Travis. Bye-bye.
Call #8Unknown+14805551234completedNew InquiryWARM
62%
85% fields▼

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_04
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments16

Summary

The homeowner called to ask whether the company handles roof trusses, explaining they already have engineering plans and need someone to perform the work. The representative clarified that their roofing company does not install or build trusses and advised the customer to contact a contractor/home builder instead. After the homeowner asked for referrals and noted that other roofing companies say they do truss repair but ultimately do not, the representative provided a referral to a construction company (described as five-star rated) and gave its phone number. The call ended without scheduling any roofing service.

• Customer asked if the company deals with roof trusses and said they already have a plan from an engineer but need someone to do the work.
• Representative stated they do not install/build trusses and recommended contacting a contractor/home builder (or architectural engineer for trusses).
• Customer is located near Westgate/Glendale, and the representative provided a referral to a construction company ("TRI-Gen Construction") with phone number 602-328-6250; no appointment was booked.

Action Items

☑ Customer to contact the referred construction company at 602-328-6250 regarding truss work.
☑ Customer to seek a contractor/home builder capable of installing trusses using the existing engineering plans.
☑ Provide the customer with the contractor referral contact details for roof truss work

Next Steps

➤ Customer calls the provided referral to request an estimate/scheduling for truss installation.
➤ If the referral cannot help, customer continues contacting a home builder/contractor that performs truss work.
Sentiment
60%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

62%
Followed (2/7)
Missed (4/7)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & Confirmation

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

medium
After learning the caller had engineered truss plans and needed installation, the rep immediately stated the company doesn’t do trusses and moved to a referral without collecting the caller’s name, ph
How to fix: Before referring out, capture basic identification and offer to send the referral details via text/email, plus invite them to call back if they need roofing services later.
"“I can point you to a couple contractors who may handle truss installs. Before I do—what’s your name and the best number to text the info to? And if you run into any issues, feel free to call us back for anything roofing-related.”"
medium
The rep did not clarify key job details (type of truss work, residential vs. addition, urgency, permit/inspection needs) and did not confirm whether the caller was the homeowner/decision maker before
How to fix: Ask 2–3 quick scoping questions before referring: what structure, what’s being replaced/repaired, timeline/urgency, and whether permits are involved.
"“Just so I point you to the right pro—are these roof trusses for a repair on an existing home or new construction/addition? Is anything currently sagging or unsafe? And how soon are you trying to get this done?”"
low
When providing the referral, the rep’s delivery was unclear (company name not confidently stated/spelled) and there was no confirmation that the caller wrote it down correctly or understood the next s
How to fix: State the company name clearly, spell it if needed, repeat the phone number, and ask for a read-back. Offer to text the details.
"“The company is TRI-Gen Construction—T-R-I dash G-E-N. The number is 602-328-6250. Want to read that back to make sure it’s right, or I can text it to you?”"
low
The call ended without a structured close: no recap of what was provided, no offer for additional help, and no confirmation of next steps beyond a quick goodbye.
How to fix: Close with a brief recap, confirm the customer’s plan, and invite them to call back if they need roofing services.
"“So to recap, we don’t install trusses, but I gave you TRI-Gen Construction at 602-328-6250. If they can’t help, call us back and I’ll try another option. Anything else I can help with today?”"

Objections Detected (1)

high Service Not Catered Resolved
"Okay. Every, every roofing place I call says trust repair, but then they, they don't, nobody does it."
💡 Best practice: after giving the referral, offer an additional backup option (another contractor/engineer contact) and confirm the customer has the full details (spelling, number) and any recommended keywords to use when calling (e.g., “truss repair/framing”).

Transcript (16 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling us on our roofers. This is Matty. How can I help you?
home_ownerHey, I was just wondering, do you guys deal with roof trusses?
customer_repno, we do not. you would need an architectural engineer for trusted.
home_ownerI have, I have the engineering, I just don't, I have, sorry, I have a plan from the engineer, I just need somebody to do it now.
customer_repyeah so we don't um we don't actually install the trusses we don't build them so if you already have the plans i would ah recommend contacting a contractor like a home builder.
home_ownerokay do you have any any on hand that you would know to call or?
customer_repah let me see here where you located.
home_ownerUh, near Westgate, so Glendo.
customer_repOne bill, okay. Thank you. Just looking at something here for you real quick.
home_ownerOkay. Every, every roofing place I call says trust repair, but then they, they don't, nobody does it.
customer_repyeah yeah yeah yeah roofing companies they usually do like above the trusses. yeah yeah roofing companies they usually do like above the trusses. the actual roof on the outside of the home. So let's see. All right, I do have a couple people for you here. Just let me know whenever you're ready.
home_ownerah i'm ready.
customer_repAlright, so there is a company called dry, like T. R. I. dash Jen. Oh, so again, yeah. Yeah, construction. Um, they are rated five stars. Their phone number is, uh, six zero two three two eight six two five zero.
home_ownerokay i'll try them thank you.
customer_repyeah, you're welcome. Right, mm-hmm, bye.
home_ownerand bye.
Call #9Ash+14805551234completedFollow UpWARM
82%
87% fields▼

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_01
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments27

Summary

The homeowner called back to speak with Maddy about the plan for repairing their roof and was transferred to her. Maddy explained the upcoming project workflow, starting with a preparation phase where a manager reviews measurements, materials, roof photos, scope of work, and colors, and may contact the homeowner if additional information is needed. She then outlined the pre-production phase where the production team builds material and work orders, assigns a crew, and completes logistical planning, after which the production team will call to confirm the homeowner’s availability and explain material delivery and what to expect during the job. The homeowner asked when “day one” would be and requested the project be expedited as much as possible, noting they have no blocked-out dates. The call ended with no further questions and the rep inviting the homeowner to call back anytime.

• Customer called to reconnect with Maddy regarding the plan for repairing their roof and was transferred to her.
• Rep explained the preparation phase: manager review of measurements, materials, roof photos, scope of work, and colors, with possible follow-up calls if more information is needed.
• Customer wants the project expedited as soon as possible and stated there are no blocker dates for scheduling.

Action Items

☑ Production team to call the homeowner within the next 5–7 business days to collect availability and review material delivery and job expectations.
☑ Internal team to note the homeowner has no blocked dates and prefers the earliest possible scheduling.
☑ Check whether Maddy is available now and transfer the customer to her if she is
☑ Provide the customer the project start timeline, including when day one is and when the project manager will come to the property
☑ Document that the customer has no blackout dates and wants the project scheduled as soon as possible, and prioritize expedited scheduling accordingly

Next Steps

➤ Manager completes the preparation-phase review of the file (measurements, materials, photos, scope, colors).
➤ After pre-production planning and the availability call, production will follow up a few days later with the official start date.

Greeting & IdentificationConfirm Project DetailsSet Expectations for Next StepsAddress Questions/ConcernsClose ProfessionallyGreeting & Identification (company name not stated; caller name not confirmed by first rep)

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
At the start, the first rep greeted with only a first name and immediately moved to a hold/transfer without stating the company name or confirming the caller’s name/contact details.
How to fix: Use a compliant greeting that includes company name, your name, and confirm the caller’s name and a callback number before placing on hold/transferring.
"“Thanks for calling [Company], this is Deva. May I get your name and the best callback number in case we get disconnected? And you’re trying to reach Maddy about your roof repair, correct?”"
medium
After the transfer, the second rep began setting expectations for the project phases without confirming the specific project details (address/job file) to ensure they were discussing the correct accou
How to fix: Before providing project updates, confirm at least two identifiers (customer name + address or job number) and restate the project scope at a high level.
"“Before I walk through next steps, can I confirm I’m looking at the right file—this is for Ash at [address], roof repair/replacement project, correct?”"
low
The hand-off language was unclear; the first rep told the caller they were transferred but did not confirm who they were connected to, and the second rep greeted with a different name than the caller
How to fix: Use a clear warm-transfer script: announce the person/department, confirm the connection, and ensure the customer knows who they’re speaking with.
"“I have Maddy available—I'm connecting you now. Maddy, I have Ash on the line calling about the roof repair plan.” Then: “Ash, you’re now connected with Maddy.”"

Objections Detected (2)

low Customer Needs Time to Decide Unresolved
"yeah, I would prefer to expedite this as soon as possible, as much as possible, and, uh, um, there are no blocker dates for, for my thing, so just keep that in mind, or if you have to put that in note"
💡 Acknowledge and take action explicitly: confirm you will document "no blocker dates" in the file, explain whether expediting is possible (and what the fastest realistic timeline is), and set a clear next step (e.g., "I’ve added that note; production should call within 5–7 business days—if you don’t hear by X date, call me and I’ll follow up").
low Customer Needs Time to Decide Resolved
"Yes, so when does, uh, when is day one basically, when does the project manager come here and we get started with the process? When does, uh, when is day one basically, when does the project manager c"
💡 Best practice: confirm the customer’s goal (expedite), restate the specific timeline (5–7 business days for the production call), and offer a clear next action (e.g., "I’ll note no blocker dates and request earliest scheduling; if you don’t hear from production by day 7, call me and I’ll follow up").

Pending Actions (0)

Show 3 rejected items
✕ [check_availability] Check whether Maddy is available now and transfer the customer to her if she is (invalid_type_no_match: check_availability)
✕ [send_details] Provide the customer the project start timeline, including when day one is and when the project mana (invalid_type_no_match: send_details)
✕ [send_details] Document that the customer has no blackout dates and wants the project scheduled as soon as possible (invalid_type_no_match: send_details)

Transcript (27 segments)

customer_repMy name is Deva, how can I help you?
home_ownerUh, hi, Jiva. I actually was in conversation with Maddy, um, regarding the plan for, uh, repairing my roof. So, do you know, do you think if she's still around or is it too late to…
customer_repYeah, let me, let me put you on a brief hold and see if she's available and I'll get you transferred over if she is, okay?
home_ownerOkay, thank you.
customer_repYeah, one moment, please stay on the line.
customer_repyou.
customer_repalrighty, you're all transferred.
home_ownerThank you.
customer_rephey ash, how are you?
home_ownerHey, Matty, thanks. Uh, I'm good, thanks. Uh sorry, I know, uh, we were trying to connect previously, but I…
customer_repoh no, you're fine. and it can work. you're fine, are you all done at work now?
home_ownerum yeah i can i can talk now um we have nine hundred percent retention. yeah yeah i know i can. Thank you.
customer_repi can ah talk to you for a sec here, just to let you know what to expect for the first phase of your project, it really shouldn't take very long.
home_ownerYeah, okay.
customer_repAlright, so the first phase of your project here with us is what we call the preparation phase. This is just where your file will go through review by one of our managers, and what they're gonna do is they're gonna verify things like your measurements, materials and photos of your roof as well as the scope of work for your job and colors. That's just what helps us make sure that your project gets over to our production team smoothly. And then if there's any extra information that we need from you, you might reach out to get that from you, so just keep an eye out for any additional phone calls from us.
home_ownerOkay.
customer_repAnd then once this manager review here is completed, that's where your job moves over to the pre-production phase. And it's just where our production team, they're going to start doing things like building out your material orders, work orders as well as completing the crew assignment for your job. And they'll also complete any color or material selection forms and logistical planning. And then once all of this, uh, preproduction stuff is done, that's when you can expect that phone call from our production team just to get your availability for your project as well as go over what to expect when we start your roof install for you. Do you have any questions about that, Ash?
home_ownerYes, so when does, uh, when is day one basically, when does the project manager come here and we get started with the process? When does, uh, when is day one basically, when does the project manager come here and we get started with the process?
customer_repYeah, so the production team will give you a phone call in the next, uh, five or seven business days, just to go over that availability with you, because we can't really get to scheduled until we know if there is, you know, certain days you can't do the project, but we can't call you for scheduling until we get your materials ordered and get a crew figured out for like who's gonna go out there and do your job. sort. Um, but once they call you for that availability and you figure out there's any dates you can't do it, and then they'll also go over, um, your material delivery with you, let you know, um, what happens with that, and they will just go over what to expect during the job, like how long the job will take, what you should expect to be going on and stuff like that. And after they get that all squared away with you, a few days after that, they'll give you that call to give you your official date. Okay. Yeah, there's just a lot that goes into getting everything ready to get scheduled.
home_ownerYeah, right, right, right. Okay. Um, yeah, I would prefer to expedite this as soon as possible, as much as possible, and, uh, um, there are no blocker dates for, for my thing, so just keep that in mind, or if you have to put that in notes, please do so, and yeah, I look forward to the pause.
customer_repalrighty ash do you have anything else for me
home_ownerUh, no, uh, I'm good.
customer_repokay well if you do have any other questions or need anything you can call me anytime.
home_ownerGreat, thank you.
customer_repyeah, you're welcome. i hope you have a great day.
home_ownerYou too, bye.
customer_repMm bye.

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_06
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone4804535721
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments6

Summary

Cindy Hannah called Arizona Roofers to return a call from Kate. Bronte answered and informed Cindy that Kate was not in yet, but said she would let Kate know that Cindy called once she arrived. The call ended politely without discussing any roofing issue, property details, or scheduling.

• Caller identified herself as Cindy Hannah and said she was returning Kate's call.
• Customer rep Bronte stated Kate was not in yet.
• Bronte said she would let Kate know Cindy called when Kate is there; no appointment or service details were discussed.

Next Steps

➤ Kate to return Cindy Hannah's call once available.
➤ If needed, gather details on the roofing request and property information during the callback.
Sentiment
78%
Summary Confidence
90%

SOP Compliance

52%
Followed (2/5)
Missed (3/5)
Greeting & IdentificationClose ProfessionallyConfirm Project DetailsSet Expectations for Next StepsAddress Questions/Concerns

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
After the caller said she was returning Kate's call, the rep only stated Kate wasn't in yet and ended the call without confirming who the caller is (full name), what the call is regarding, or the best
How to fix: Use a quick message-taking checklist: confirm full name, phone number, address/project reference, reason for the call, and best time to reach them. Then summarize the message back to the caller.
"“Absolutely—I can take a message for Kate. Can I get your full name, the best callback number, and what the call is regarding? Also, what’s the best time for her to reach you today?”"
medium
The rep did not set any expectation for when Kate would be available or when the customer should expect a return call, and did not offer an alternative (transfer to voicemail, another team member, or
How to fix: Provide a clear timeframe and options: offer to route to voicemail, schedule a callback window, or connect to someone who can help now if urgent.
"“Kate should be in by 9:30. I can have her call you back this morning—would that work, or would you prefer a call after 1 PM? If it’s urgent, I can also connect you with another coordinator.”"
low
The rep did not ask if the caller had any questions or needed help with anything else before ending the call.
How to fix: Add a brief closing check-in to capture immediate needs and prevent additional calls.
"“Before you go, is there anything I can help with right now—any questions about your project or the next steps?”"

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_03
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone6198760657
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments5

Summary

The caller asked whether they were speaking with the company owner or marketing manager and said they wanted to discuss a deal with the company. The representative (Nika) responded by requesting that the caller send an email instead. The caller acknowledged this but then asked to wait/hold on, and the call ended without any service details, scheduling, or resolution.

• Caller asked if they were speaking with the owner or marketing manager.
• Caller stated they wanted to discuss a deal with the company.
• Representative asked the caller to send an email instead; no appointment or roofing service request was discussed.

Next Steps

➤ Caller to send an email to the company to discuss the proposed deal.
Sentiment
55%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

20%
Followed (1/7)
Missed (6/7)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & ConfirmationObjection Handling

AI Coaching Recommendations (2)

high
When the caller asked, "is this the owner or marketing manager?" and said they wanted to "discuss a deal with the company," the rep immediately responded, "can you just kindly, uh, send us over an ema
How to fix: Acknowledge the request, clarify what type of deal it is, collect key details (name, company, phone/email), and either warm-transfer to the appropriate department/manager or provide the correct direct contact while confirming follow-up expectations.
"“I can help get you to the right person. May I get your name and company? What type of deal are you looking to discuss—marketing partnership, vendor services, or something else? What’s the best email and phone number to reach you? If our marketing manager is available I can transfer you; if not, I’ll send your details for a callback today.”"
medium
The rep did not complete basic identification steps (caller name and contact info) at any point, even after the caller indicated a business purpose for calling.
How to fix: Before redirecting or ending the call, capture minimum contact fields and summarize next steps (who will respond and when).
"“Before you go, can I grab your name, phone number, and email so we can make sure the right person gets back to you? What’s the best time to reach you?”"
