Call Details

Call IDreport_test_848e30ad_02
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments6

Summary

The caller asked to speak with the owner or marketing manager to discuss a deal with the company. The representative (Nika) did not transfer the call and instead requested that the caller send an email. The caller acknowledged the request but hesitated and asked to wait, and the call ended without any service details, scheduling, or resolution beyond the email request.

• Caller asked if they were speaking with the owner or marketing manager.
• Caller stated they wanted to discuss a deal with the company.
• Representative asked the caller to send an email instead of continuing the discussion on the call.

Action Items

☑ Provide the caller with the appropriate email address to send their proposal/deal details.
☑ If an email is received, route it to the owner or marketing manager for review and follow-up.
☑ Send an email to the company with deal details for review

Next Steps

➤ Caller sends an email with the deal/proposal information.
➤ Company reviews the email and responds to the caller with a decision or request for more details.
Sentiment
55%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

15%
Followed (1/7)
Missed (6/7)
Greeting & Identification (partial)Needs DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & ConfirmationObjection Handling

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
The rep did not state the company name, did not ask for the caller’s name, and did not verify contact details; the rep opened with only a first name and location.
How to fix: Use a standard greeting that includes company name, rep name, and immediately capture the caller’s name and best callback number/email before proceeding.
"“Thanks for calling [Company Name], this is Nika. May I have your name? And what’s the best phone number and email in case we get disconnected?”"
high
When the caller asked if they were speaking to the owner or marketing manager, the rep responded with an unclear statement instead of clarifying role and routing the call appropriately.
How to fix: Acknowledge the question, clarify your role, and offer to connect/route or take a message with specifics (topic, company, best contact info).
"“I’m not the owner, I’m on the customer care team. Are you calling about a marketing partnership or another type of deal? I can route you to the right person—what’s your name and the best number/email?”"
high
After the caller said they wanted to discuss a deal with the company, the rep immediately asked them to send an email and did not gather any details or provide a clear next step (who to email, what to
How to fix: Collect key details (caller name, company, purpose, urgency), provide the correct email/contact, and confirm next steps and expected response time.
"“Absolutely—what company are you with and what’s the deal about? I’ll make sure it gets to the right person. You can email [address], and I’ll also note it here. What’s the best number to reach you, and when do you need a response?”"

Objections Detected (2)

low Other Unresolved
"Okay, um, wait, hold on, hold on."
💡 Best practice: acknowledge the hesitation and clarify next steps. For example: "Of course—what’s holding you up? If you tell me briefly what the deal is about, I can route you to the right person, or I can give you the correct email and subject line to use." Then confirm commitment: "Can you send that now while I’m on the line?"
low Other Unresolved
"Uh, is this the owner or marketing manager?"
💡 Directly answer the question by identifying your role and offering the correct point of contact (e.g., "I’m Nika with the marketing team; our owner isn’t available, but I can connect you with our marketing manager or take your details now. What’s the deal about?"). Then confirm the customer is okay proceeding.

Call #13Chinh Pham+14805551234completedNew InquiryHOT

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_696c8ac0de10
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments53

Summary

The homeowner called Arizona Roofers to ask about certified shingle roof installation and to get an estimate because their shingle roof is leaking and they are considering replacing it. The representative confirmed the roof type, collected the customer’s address, name, phone number, permission to send text updates, and email for sending a written proposal. The homeowner stated the roof was originally a 30-year roof and is now about 20 years old, and confirmed there are no solar panels (but mentioned an air conditioning unit on the roof). The rep asked about HOA status (homeowner said “as I know, okay,” indicating uncertainty) and scheduled an on-site visit for Thursday, February 12th from 1–3 PM, explaining the customer would receive a confirmation text and a 30-minute pre-arrival call/text from the technician. The homeowner expressed concern about prior workmanship and requested a certified technician; the rep reassured them that leaks after 15–20 years can be normal for shingle roofs and that the company’s team is certified and established, then confirmed the appointment.

• Customer is a homeowner with a shingle roof that is leaking and wants an estimate to replace the roof.
• Roof age discussed: customer said it was a 30-year roof and is currently around 20 years old; customer also stated there are no solar panels and mentioned an air conditioning unit on the roof.
• Appointment was booked for an on-site visit on Thursday, February 12th, 1–3 PM; customer agreed to text updates and provided an email for the written proposal.

Action Items

☑ Dispatch a certified technician to the property for the scheduled inspection/estimate on Thursday, February 12th between 1–3 PM.
☑ Send confirmation text and ensure the technician provides a call/text about 30 minutes before arrival; send the written proposal to the provided Yahoo email after the visit.
☑ Perform the on-site roof inspection on Thursday during the 1–3pm window and provide recommendations

Next Steps

➤ Technician visits the property during the scheduled window to assess the leak and provide replacement recommendations and pricing.
➤ Company sends the written proposal to the customer’s email and follows up via phone/text as needed to proceed with replacement if approved.
Sentiment
72%
Summary Confidence
78%

SOP Compliance

72%
Followed (4/7)
Missed (3/7)
Greeting & IdentificationScheduling (basic)Setting Expectations (basic)Close & Confirmation (partial)Needs Discovery (depth)Qualifying (BANT)Objection Handling (quality)

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

medium
After the customer stated they have a leaking roof and want a cost estimate, the rep quickly pivoted to collecting address/contact info and scheduling without clarifying the problem details (how long
How to fix: Before scheduling, ask 3–5 targeted discovery questions to assess urgency and scope, then route appropriately (standard inspection vs. emergency). Document answers for the technician.
"“I can definitely get someone out. Before I book it, a couple quick questions: Is the leak active right now? How long has it been happening? Have you noticed any ceiling stains or interior damage? Has anyone attempted repairs or inspected it yet? And is it over a specific area like a vent or valley?”"
medium
The rep did not perform basic qualifying (BANT): no questions about timeline/urgency for replacement, whether they are getting other quotes, or any budget/financing considerations; only confirmed home
How to fix: Add lightweight qualifying questions after identifying the issue and before/after scheduling: decision makers, desired timeline, and whether they’re comparing bids; offer financing if appropriate.
"“Are you looking to repair the leak or are you thinking full replacement? Ideally, when would you like to have the work completed? Are you planning to get a couple other quotes as well? If it helps, we do have financing options—would you like info on that?”"
high
When the customer expressed concern about wanting a certified technician and fear of prior bad installation, the rep responded with a confusing challenge (“What do you mean?”) and then minimized the c
How to fix: Acknowledge the concern, validate it, then provide specific credibility points (licenses, manufacturer certifications, insured, inspection steps, photos, written scope) and invite questions.
"“That’s a fair concern—roof leaks are frustrating. Yes, our inspectors/installers are licensed and insured, and we follow manufacturer installation standards. At the visit, we’ll do a full inspection, take photos, show you exactly where the leak is coming from, and provide a written proposal with options so you can decide confidently.”"
medium
The rep did not clearly confirm key appointment details back to the customer (repeat the full address, confirm the date and time window cleanly, and confirm who will be present/access considerations).
How to fix: Perform a structured confirmation: restate date, time window, address, best phone, and access/attendance requirements; ask for explicit confirmation.
"“Perfect—so we’re set for Thursday, February 12th, between 1:00 and 3:00 PM at [repeat full address]. Is that correct? Will you be there to provide access, and are there any gate codes or pets we should know about?”"

Objections Detected (4)

medium In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"perhaps over, but, uh, I want to estimate if a good deal, I can replay them."
💡 Acknowledge the cost concern and explain the estimate process and any inspection/visit fees (if any), what the proposal includes, and what factors drive price; then confirm: "Does that help on the cost/good-deal question?"
medium Other Unresolved
"Okay, I'd like to certify people sometime, uh, not, uh, come up to, uh, take a look at something. I'd like to certified people, uh, traditionally come to take a look and suggest, uh, what he will, uh,"
💡 Directly confirm the technician will be certified and specify what that means (licenses, manufacturer certifications), offer to send credentials/ID info by text/email, and explain the inspection/proposal process to prevent repeat problems; then ask for confirmation that this addresses their concern.
medium In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"Yes, I have a licking, so I want to notice how much cost."
💡 Acknowledge the desire for a price, explain that accurate pricing requires an on-site inspection (measurements, leak source, decking/underlayment condition), and give a rough range if allowed. Then confirm: "Does that work if we have a tech out Thursday to give you a written quote?"
medium In-Person Estimates Only Resolved
"Can somebody can stop by and take a look and see how much cost to replay the new one?"
💡 Best practice: confirm the goal (repair vs full replacement) and set expectations that the technician will inspect, discuss options, and provide a written proposal/price after the visit.

Transcript (53 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Roofers. This is Bronte. How can I help you?
home_ownerOh, hi. I checked on the website and I see your guy have a certified, uh, roping installation for, um, um, for single to roping.
customer_repYeah, do you have a, a shingle roof?
home_owneryeah ah i have a.
customer_repokay well.
home_ownerYes, I have a licking, so I want to notice how much cost. Can somebody can stop by and take a look and see how much cost to replay the new one?
customer_repYeah, give me one second.
home_ownerOkay,
customer_repOkay, what's your property address?
home_ownerUh, one three zero one West L. N. R. E. L. N. A. C. R. A. E. uh, street Pambi A. five to A. one.
customer_repOkay, give me just one second.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repOkay, and what is your first and last name?
home_ownerMy foot name, C-H-I-N-H, not name form, not name form P-S-A-M.
customer_repOkay. And are you the homeowner?
home_owneryeah.
customer_repOkay, is this phone number the best way to reach you?
home_owneryeah.
customer_repOkay. Can I send you text updates?
home_ownerSure.
customer_repOkay. And then what's the best email for you? That's how we'll send over the written proposal.
home_ownerC underscore form P H A M 35 35 at yahoo dot com.
customer_repOkay. And how old is the roof?
home_ownerUh, the roof, uh, you install, uh, the roof a 30-year, but, uh, just, uh, right now only around 20-year, but uh, around acquisition, I think you have licking, I already have a…
customer_repOkay. perhaps over, but, uh, I want to estimate if a good deal, I can replay them.
customer_repOkay. And, um, is the property in an HOA community?
home_ownerUh, as so I know, okay.
customer_repAnd do you have any solar panels on the roof?
home_ownerUh, no.
customer_repOkay.
home_ownerI have, uh, air conditioning on the roof.
customer_repOkay. All right, I can have somebody out to you, uh, this Thursday, February 12th at 1 p.m., is that? Is that? work for you?
home_ownerI'll go back. Oh, what's that?
customer_repOkay, I said, uh, uh, Thursday, uh, okay, um, you said one o'clock? One to three PM, does that work for you?
home_ownerOh, okay, yeah, one, two, three, five. Oh.
customer_repokay. I'll book that for you and then once we're off this phone call, you'll get a confirmation text with the appointment details and then the day of your appointment. one. Your technician will give you a text or a phone call when he's about thirty minutes away from the property so you know exactly when to expect him.
home_ownerUh, I have a question ma'am, um, I'd like to have a…
customer_repYeah.
home_ownerUh, before you do the work, um, I don't know if you do something wrong or what, so I need to certify the technician to install, to take a look and see what he suggests. I don't want to, um, have problem again.
customer_repWhat do you mean? what do you mean?
home_ownerUh, okay, the group, there are people installed for me before, uh, 30-year, but the 50-year, they already have a leaking, so maybe when the people before you do something not correct?
customer_repUh, no, that's normal for- Uh, no, that's normal, sure. That's, that's totally normal for shingle roofs to start leaking after about fifteen to twenty years.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repYeah, and everyone here is certified. Yeah, and everyone. Um, we're licensed under the BBB, um, if you'd like to look us up at the Better Business Bureau, you can, we're all…
home_ownerMm-hmm. Okay.
home_ownerOkay, I'd like to certify people sometime, uh, not, uh, come up to, uh, take a look at something. I'd like to certified people, uh, traditionally come to take a look and suggest, uh, what he will, uh, do the best for my group.
customer_repYeah, we've, we've been in business for about twenty-eight years, so no one's gonna steer you wrong.
home_ownerOkay.
customer_repAll right. So we'll be on Thursday from one to three. Yep, and they'll give you a text before they arrive.
home_ownerSure, thank you very much, Matt.
customer_repOf course, have a good day too.
home_ownerAlright, thank you very much. Bye bye.
customer_repbye bye.

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_1f919b14fa5e
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments47

Summary

Jennifer Barker from Optimum/Optical Medical called Arizona Roofers after emailing info@arizonaroofers earlier on 2026-03-02, requesting a roof inspection for a commercial property at 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ 85374 that is being converted from a former bank into a primary care clinic. She reported that contractors recently found a couple of roof leaks over the last few days and asked if someone could inspect as soon as tomorrow (2026-03-03). Steve explained he could not guarantee next-day availability but took her contact and property details and said a technician would reach out to coordinate a date/time, perform a full inspection with photos and a report, and send proposals via email; Jennifer preferred text updates and confirmed contractors should be on site for the inspection.

• Customer reported a couple of roof leaks discovered over the last couple of days at a commercial building being converted from a bank into a primary care medical clinic and requested an inspection as soon as 2026-03-03.
• Property details provided: 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ 85374; single-story building; customer described it as a flat roof (roof age unknown).
• Outcome: Rep could not guarantee a next-day visit, collected Jennifer Barker’s contact info and said a technician will call to coordinate the inspection; customer opted for text message updates and indicated contractors should be on site.

Action Items

☑ Assign the lead to a technician and have them contact Jennifer Barker at 909-684-6098 to schedule the roof inspection.
☑ Send inspection/proposal communications to Jennifer at jbarker@optimaZ.com (as spelled on the call) and provide text updates as requested.

Next Steps

➤ Technician to coordinate an inspection appointment with Jennifer and confirm that someone will be on site during the visit.
➤ After inspection, provide the photo documentation and written report with options/proposal to the customer via email.
Sentiment
78%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

78%
Followed (5/7)
Missed (2/7)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT partial)Setting ExpectationsClose & Confirmation (partial)Scheduling (no appointment set/confirmed)Objection Handling (not applicable—no objections raised)

AI Coaching Recommendations (6)

high
After the customer asked for an inspection 'tomorrow,' the rep did not offer available time windows or book a tentative appointment; instead, he said he would 'give it to my technician and he can coor
How to fix: Attempt to schedule during the call by offering 2–3 specific time windows (even if tentative) and confirm who will be on-site. If scheduling must be handled by a technician, set a firm callback SLA (e.g., within 30–60 minutes) and confirm the best times to reach the customer.
"“I can’t guarantee tomorrow yet, but I can request it. Would tomorrow 9–11am or 1–3pm work if we can fit you in? If not, what’s your next best day? Either way, our technician will call you within the next hour to confirm the exact time.”"
medium
The rep did not ask key urgency and problem-scope questions (when the leaks started, whether there is active water intrusion/damage, where the leaks are occurring, or if anyone has inspected already)
How to fix: Right after the customer mentions leaks, ask a short set of triage questions to determine severity and urgency, then tailor scheduling and expectations accordingly.
"“When did you first notice the leaks? Is water actively coming in right now or causing interior damage? Has anyone taken a look yet, and do you know roughly where on the roof it’s happening?”"
low
Early in the call, the rep responded with confusion and an abrupt tone when clarifying the email address, which could come across as unprofessional.
How to fix: Acknowledge the email calmly and confirm details with a polite, structured question.
"“Thanks for sending that—just to confirm, you emailed info@arizonaroofers.com, correct? Great. Let’s get this scheduled.”"
medium
The rep collected address, email, and phone but did not read them back to confirm accuracy (especially important given spelling and numbers).
How to fix: Repeat back the address, email, and phone number and ask for confirmation before ending the call.
"“Let me read that back to make sure I have it right: 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise 85374, and your cell is 909-684-6098, email jbarker@optima… Is that correct?”"
medium
Qualifying was partial: the rep confirmed ownership but did not clarify the decision/approval process, timeline for repairs, or whether other bids are being collected.
How to fix: Ask 2–3 quick BANT questions tailored to commercial: timeline/urgency, decision makers/approval, and whether they’re comparing bids.
"“What’s your target timeline to have the leaks addressed before the clinic opens? Besides you, who else needs to approve the proposal? Are you getting other roofing quotes as well?”"
low
The close did not set a clear next-step timeframe or confirmation method (e.g., when the technician will call, and whether a confirmation text/email will be sent).
How to fix: End with a concise recap and a specific follow-up commitment (timeframe) plus confirmation channel.
"“Next step: our technician will call you within 60 minutes to confirm the inspection time. Once it’s booked, we’ll text you the appointment details. If you don’t hear from us by then, call us at [number].”"

Objections Detected (2)

low Scheduling Conflicts Unresolved
"I have a location in surprise that we are currently in process of making into a clinic, and over the last couple of days, they found some roof leaks, and I'm looking to see if you guys have somebody a"
💡 Directly address the scheduling need by offering concrete options and confirming one: e.g., "I can’t guarantee tomorrow, but I can offer tomorrow afternoon between X–Y or first thing the next morning—what works best?" If tomorrow is critical, offer an escalation/priority note and set a clear follow-up time for the technician to call.
low Other Resolved
"I am. no idea. And that's a… Unfortunately, I have very, I have very little information about the, about the, um, the age of the roof or anything like that."
💡 Best practice: reassure explicitly that not knowing the roof age is common and not required for an inspection (e.g., 'No problem—our tech can estimate age/condition during the inspection and focus on locating the leak sources').

summary.summary✅PresentJennifer Barker from Optimum/Optical Medical called Arizona Roofers after emailing info@arizonaroofe

Transcript (47 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Roofers. My name is Steve. How can I help you out?
home_ownerMy name is Jennifer Barker. I'm with Optical Medical, and I sent an email a little bit earlier today. I have a site enterprise that
customer_repWhat? Oh. You send an email to… You think- What, you sent the email to what email, sorry? The email to what email first?
home_ownerThe info at Arizona Roofers.
home_ownerOkay. I have a location in surprise that we are currently in process of making into a clinic, and over the last couple of days, they found some roof leaks, and I'm looking to see if you guys have somebody available tomorrow that can do an inspection for me.
customer_repDo you know how old the roof is?
home_ownerI am. no idea. And that's a… Unfortunately, I have very, I have very little information about the, about the, um, the age of the roof or anything like that.
customer_repOkay, and this is a commercial property.
home_ownerIt is.
customer_repOkay, I'm not sure if I can guarantee someone coming out there tomorrow, um, but we could take your information down and I can give it to my technician and he can coordinate a time and date, um, MS, do you own the property or are you a project?
home_ownerWe do own it. We do own it, yes.
customer_repGotcha. And what type of a clinic is it?
home_ownerWell, it's a primary care medical clinic. Okay. But it was, it was a bank and we're converting it into a clinic.
customer_repGotcha, and you're just experiencing that, those couple of leaks on-
home_ownerYes.
customer_repOkay, let me grab your information down and then I will have a technician reach out to you. Um, can I start with the property address?
home_ownerSure, it's one, three, nine, two, six, West Bell Road, in surprise, and it's eight, five, three, seven, four.
customer_repNo. You're gonna be the point of-
home_ownerI am the main contact, yes. I will not be on Facebook.
customer_repOkay, can I get yours? Okay, is someone gonna be on site? We do require someone to be on site during time of inspection, preferably.
home_ownerI believe I believe we do have, um, our contractors there. You have, um, our contractors there.
customer_repOkay, you don't have any decision makers that aren't gonna be able to be on site, like any owners or managers or anything like that?
home_ownerNo, not that I'm aware of, no.
customer_repOkay. Okay. Can I grab your first and last name?
home_ownerSure. It's Jennifer Barker, and it's B. S. A. R. K. E. R. .
customer_repAnd Jennifer, what's your role there? Are you, uh, just… Okay. And so… Thank you. here. Okay, perfect. Can I grab your email, Jennifer?
home_ownerSure, it's J. Barker, again, B. S. P. L. A. R. K. E. R., at optimal. After… That's O. P. is in Paul. T. is in Tom. I. M. A. Mm-hmm. A is in apple, Z is in zebra dot com.
customer_repOkay. Then the- The last phone number to reach you?
home_ownerUh, my cell phone is nine zero nine six eight four six zero nine eight.
customer_repOkay. Am I able to send you email and text message updates about the roof?
home_ownerText message update. Yeah.
customer_repOkay. Do you know what type of roofing material is on the roof?
home_ownerTile should color flat. Uh, it's a flat roof. But okay.
customer_repIs this a single story, uh, building?
home_ownerYes. Mm-hmm.
customer_repDo you know how big it is, square feet-wise?
home_ownerI don't have information straight off the end, but I can probably figure it out, but I don't have it with me.
customer_repOkay, no worries. Um, is there any gate codes we should be aware of?
home_ownerand not that i'm aware of, no.
customer_repOkay, I'm assuming there's no HOA since it's commercial, and who do, who owns the property?
home_ownerUh, Optimum does.
customer_repAnd then I'm assuming we're gonna be sending, so we'll have the technician coordinate with you and then still be sending all that information to your email, like proposals and stuff.
home_ownerMm-hmm.
customer_repOkay. All right, I'll go ahead and let my technician know, and he'll reach out with you with the time and day that works for both of you. Um, how it kind of works is when they come out there, you'll be receiving, uh, when they come out here, they do a full roof inspection, they take about fifty to a hundred photos, we write a full report, um, with all of your options, and then I know if you guys choose to go with the same day, they do take some money off the top as well. But if you don't have any other questions, Jennifer, I, you'll be getting reached out to,
home_ownerNow, I appreciate that. Thank you so much.
customer_repOf course, no problem. Have a good day.
home_ownerYou too.
Call #15Unknown6198760657completedNew InquiryUNQUALIFIED
18%
64% fields▼

Call Details

Call IDtest-fix-sandiego-002
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone6198760657
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments6

Summary

The caller asked to speak with the owner or marketing manager to discuss a deal with the company. The representative (Nika) did not transfer the call and instead requested that the caller send an email. The caller acknowledged the request but paused the conversation with “wait, hold on,” and the call ended without any service details, scheduling, or resolution.

• Caller asked if they were speaking with the owner or marketing manager.
• Caller stated they wanted to discuss a deal with the company.
• Representative asked the caller to send an email instead of continuing on the phone; no appointment or service request was made.

Next Steps

➤ Caller to email the company with details about the proposed deal.
Sentiment
55%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

18%
Followed (1/7)
Missed (5/7)
Greeting & Identification (partial)Needs DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & Confirmation

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

high
After the caller asked, "is this the owner or marketing manager?" the rep responded unclearly ("Uh, we know the reason why.") and then quickly pushed the caller to email ("can you just kindly, uh, sen
How to fix: Acknowledge the request, clarify what they need, and offer a clear routing path. Collect name, company, callback/email, and set an expectation for who will respond and when. If you must redirect to email, provide the exact address and confirm you’ll forward it internally.
"“I’m not the owner, but I can help get you to the right person. May I get your name and company? What’s the deal you’re looking to discuss—marketing partnership, vendor services, or something else? What’s the best email and phone number to reach you? I can forward this to our marketing manager today and you should hear back within 1 business day. You can also email details to marketing@company.com—would you like me to repeat that?”"
medium
The rep did not complete Greeting & Identification beyond a first name/location and did not ask for the caller’s name or any contact details before ending the interaction.
How to fix: Immediately after the greeting, ask for the caller’s name and best callback/email, then confirm it back. Even for non-service inquiries, capture contact details before transferring or redirecting.
"“Absolutely—before I connect you or take a message, may I get your name and the best phone number/email to reach you?”"
medium
No close and confirmation occurred: the rep did not provide an email address, did not confirm what to include in the email, and did not set a follow-up timeframe; the caller ended with hesitation ("wa
How to fix: Summarize the request, give the exact next step (email/transfer), confirm the details to include, and set a response SLA. Ask if there’s anything else before ending.
"“Please email your proposal to partnerships@company.com with your name, company, and a quick summary. I’ll forward it today, and someone will respond by tomorrow. Is there anything you need from us in the meantime?”"

Call #16Unknown4036931546completedFresh SalesUNQUALIFIED
20%
64% fields▼

Call Details

Call IDtest-fix-clemence-001
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone4036931546
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments10

Summary

Lisa from Arizona Rufus answered the call and informed the caller she does not speak Spanish. She then asked to speak with Camilla and confirmed she was speaking with Maria Ramirez. Lisa stated she would request a callback with the company’s production team, and the call ended without any roofing issue details, property information, or scheduling being discussed.

• The representative stated she does not speak Spanish and apologized.
• The representative confirmed the caller’s name as Maria Ramirez.
• The representative said she would request a callback with the production team; no service details or appointment were set on the call.

Next Steps

➤ Production team to contact Maria Ramirez by phone to determine the reason for the call and needed roofing service.
➤ During the callback, collect property/service details and schedule the appropriate next appointment if needed.
Sentiment
55%
Summary Confidence
78%

SOP Compliance

20%
Followed (1/7)
Missed (5/7)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & Confirmation

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After greeting, the rep did not ask what the customer needed and moved quickly to routing a callback to the production team without understanding the request.
How to fix: Pause routing and complete basic needs discovery first (issue type, urgency, property basics). Then route/schedule based on what’s learned.
"“I can help with that. What’s going on with the roof today—are you seeing an active leak or damage? How long has it been happening, and what’s your address so I can get you to the right next step?”"
high
The rep acknowledged they don’t speak Spanish but did not offer a Spanish-speaking option or interpreter and did not confirm the customer could proceed in English.
How to fix: Use a clear language-assistance workflow: offer a Spanish-speaking representative or interpreter line, and confirm the preferred language before continuing.
"“I’m sorry—I don’t speak Spanish, but I can connect you with a Spanish-speaking teammate right now. Would you like to continue in Spanish or English?”"
medium
No scheduling or concrete next-step commitment was made; the rep did not provide a callback timeframe or confirm best phone number/email for follow-up.
How to fix: Set a specific expectation for the callback window and verify the best callback number (and email/text preference).
"“I’m going to have our team call you back within the next 30–60 minutes. Is this the best number to reach you, and would you like a text confirmation as well?”"
medium
The call ended without a clear close/confirmation (no recap of what will happen next, no confirmation of the customer’s request, and no invitation for questions).
How to fix: End with a brief recap, confirm next steps, and provide a way to reach the company if needed.
"“Just to confirm, Maria, I’ve requested a callback from our team. They’ll reach out within an hour. If you miss the call, you can call us back at [number]. Is there anything else I can help with right now?”"

Call #17Ash+14805551234completedFollow UpWARM
62%
86% fields▼

Call Details

Call IDstaging_run_f24b8c19aa36
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments21

Summary

The homeowner called back to reconnect with Maddy about the plan for repairing their roof and was transferred to her. Maddy explained the upcoming project workflow: a preparation phase where a manager reviews measurements, materials, roof photos, scope of work, and colors, followed by a pre-production phase where the production team builds material/work orders, assigns a crew, completes selection forms, and plans logistics. The homeowner asked when “day one” would be and Maddy said the production team should call within the next 5–7 business days to confirm availability and review material delivery and job expectations, then provide an official start date a few days after that. The homeowner requested the process be expedited as much as possible and stated they have no blocked dates, and the call ended with no further questions.

• Customer is in an ongoing conversation about a roof repair plan and called to speak with Maddy; the call was transferred to her.
• Rep outlined the process: manager review (measurements, materials, roof photos, scope of work, colors) followed by pre-production (material/work orders, crew assignment, selection forms, logistics).
• Customer wants the project expedited and stated there are no blocker dates; rep said production will call in the next 5–7 business days to discuss availability and later provide an official date.

Action Items

☑ Production team to call the customer within 5–7 business days to confirm availability, review material delivery, and set expectations for the job.
☑ Add/ensure notes on the account that the customer has no blocked dates and wants the project expedited as much as possible.
☑ Check whether Maddy is available now and transfer the customer to her if available

Next Steps

➤ Complete manager review in the preparation phase (verify measurements, materials, photos, scope of work, and colors).
➤ Move the job into pre-production to finalize orders and crew assignment, then schedule and provide the official start date after the availability call.
Sentiment
75%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

62%
Followed (4/5)
Missed (1/5)
Greeting & IdentificationSet Expectations for Next StepsAddress Questions/ConcernsClose ProfessionallyConfirm Project Details

AI Coaching Recommendations (2)

medium
After the transfer, the rep launched into process expectations without first confirming the customer’s project details (e.g., which roof job, current status, and any key selections already made).
How to fix: Before explaining next steps, confirm the job and current status in 1–2 questions, then tailor expectations. Verify the customer’s name and the project identifier (address) and confirm what has already been decided (materials/colors) and where the file is in the workflow.
"“Before I walk through next steps, can I confirm I’m speaking with Ash and this is for the roof project at [address]? And just to confirm, we’re at the stage right after the agreement—materials/colors are [X], correct?”"
low
At the start of the call, the rep did not state the company name and did not verify the caller’s identity/contact information before proceeding with assistance and transfer.
How to fix: Open with company + name, then verify the caller’s name and at least one identifier (phone/email/address) before discussing project specifics or transferring.
"“Thanks for calling [Company], this is Deva. May I have your name and the address on the project so I can pull up the right file before I connect you with Maddy?”"

summary.summary✅PresentThe homeowner called back to reconnect with Maddy about the plan for repairing their roof and was tr

Transcript (21 segments)

customer_repMy name is Deva, how can I help you?
home_ownerUh, hi, Jiva. I actually was in conversation with Maddy, um, regarding the plan for, uh, repairing my roof. So, do you know, do you think if she's still around or is it too late to…
customer_repYeah, let me, let me put you on a brief hold and see if she's available and I'll get you transferred over if she is, okay?
home_ownerOkay, thank you.
customer_repYeah, one moment, please stay on the line. you. alrighty, you're all transferred.
home_ownerThank you.
customer_rephey ash, how are you?
home_ownerHey, Matty, thanks. Uh, I'm good, thanks. Uh sorry, I know, uh, we were trying to connect previously, but I…
customer_repoh no, you're fine. and it can work. you're fine, are you all done at work now?
home_ownerum yeah i can i can talk now um we have nine hundred percent retention. yeah yeah i know i can. Thank you. i can ah talk to you for a sec here, just to let you know what to expect for the first phase of your project, it really shouldn't take very long.
customer_repYeah, okay.
home_ownerAlright, so the first phase of your project here with us is what we call the preparation phase. This is just where your file will go through review by one of our managers, and what they're gonna do is they're gonna verify things like your measurements, materials and photos of your roof as well as the scope of work for your job and colors. That's just what helps us make sure that your project gets over to our production team smoothly. And then if there's any extra information that we need from you, you might reach out to get that from you, so just keep an eye out for any additional phone calls from us. Okay. And then once this manager review here is completed, that's where your job moves over to the pre-production phase. And it's just where our production team, they're going to start doing things like building out your material orders, work orders as well as completing the crew assignment for your job. And they'll also complete any color or material selection forms and logistical planning. And then once all of this, uh, preproduction stuff is done, that's when you can expect that phone call from our production team just to get your availability for your project as well as go over what to expect when we start your roof install for you. Do you have any questions about that, Ash?
customer_repYes, so when does, uh, when is day one basically, when does the project manager come here and we get started with the process? When does, uh, when is day one basically, when does the project manager come here and we get started with the process?
home_ownerYeah, so the production team will give you a phone call in the next, uh, five or seven business days, just to go over that availability with you, because we can't really get to scheduled until we know if there is, you know, certain days you can't do the project, but we can't call you for scheduling until we get your materials ordered and get a crew figured out for like who's gonna go out there and do your job. sort. Um, but once they call you for that availability and you figure out there's any dates you can't do it, and then they'll also go over, um, your material delivery with you, let you know, um, what happens with that, and they will just go over what to expect during the job, like how long the job will take, what you should expect to be going on and stuff like that. And after they get that all squared away with you, a few days after that, they'll give you that call to give you your official date. Okay. Yeah, there's just a lot that goes into getting everything ready to get scheduled.
customer_repYeah, right, right, right. Okay. Um, yeah, I would prefer to expedite this as soon as possible, as much as possible, and, uh, um, there are no blocker dates for, for my thing, so just keep that in mind, or if you have to put that in notes, please do so, and yeah, I look forward to the pause.
home_owneralrighty ash do you have anything else for me
customer_repUh, no, uh, I'm good.
home_ownerokay well if you do have any other questions or need anything you can call me anytime.
customer_repGreat, thank you.
home_owneryeah, you're welcome. i hope you have a great day.
customer_repYou too, bye. Mm bye.

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_fb6fb8826c4f
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments7

Summary

The caller, who is in the process of buying a home currently in escrow, said a full home inspection was completed on 2026-03-02 and they now need a roof inspection as soon as possible. The representative confirmed they likely have availability to get someone out ASAP and explained that for real estate transactions there is a one-time $3.99 fee that would be applied toward any needed repairs or replacement. The caller stated they were calling around for prices, chose not to schedule during the call, and said they would call back after making a few more calls if it works out.

• Customer is buying a home in escrow and requested a roof inspection after a full inspection on 2026-03-02 indicated one was needed.
• Customer asked for someone to come out ASAP; the rep said they likely have availability to get out there ASAP.
• Rep disclosed a one-time $3.99 fee for real estate transaction inspections that would go toward repairs and/or replacement; customer did not book and plans to call back after comparing prices.

Action Items

☑ Be prepared to schedule a roof inspection quickly if the customer calls back (requested ASAP).
☑ If the customer calls back, restate the $3.99 one-time real estate transaction fee and that it applies toward repairs/replacement.

Next Steps

➤ Customer will make additional price-comparison calls and may call back to schedule.
➤ Company to await customer callback; no appointment was set during this call.
Sentiment
62%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

42%
Followed (2/6)
Missed (4/6)
Greeting & IdentificationAttempt to schedule (initial attempt)Understand the NeedEDUCATE on need for in-person assessmentHandle objections about inspectionClose (with next steps/recapture)

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

medium
After the homeowner explained they are in escrow and need a roof inspection ASAP, the rep moved directly to quoting a transaction fee and scheduling without asking any discovery questions (address, ro
How to fix: Pause and ask 3–5 targeted discovery questions before discussing fees/scheduling, then summarize and transition to the inspection appointment.
"“Got it—since you’re in escrow, we can help quickly. A couple quick questions so we send the right person: What did the home inspection note about the roof? Any active leaks or interior staining? What’s the property address and roof type (tile/shingle/flat)? And what’s your deadline for the escrow contingency?”"
high
The rep did not explain WHY an in-person roof inspection is needed; they only stated a fee and offered to schedule.
How to fix: Provide a concise value-based explanation that pricing depends on roof type, pitch, layers, damage extent, and repairability, and that the inspection produces documentation useful for escrow negotiations.
"“We can’t give an accurate roof quote over the phone because the cost depends on what we find—tile condition, underlayment, flashing, and any hidden damage. The inspection lets us document everything and give you a written report/estimate you can use for your escrow negotiations.”"
high
When the customer said they were calling around for prices and would call back, the rep accepted the brush-off without probing, value framing, offering options, or attempting to secure a tentative app
How to fix: Acknowledge, differentiate, and offer a low-friction next step: hold a same/next-day slot, explain what they get, and ask a closing question. If they still won’t book, capture contact info and set a specific follow-up time.
"“Totally understand. Since you need this ASAP for escrow, would it help if I hold our earliest slot while you compare? The inspection includes a written roof condition report and repair/replacement options. What’s the best email/number to send details, and can I pencil you in for tomorrow morning or afternoon?”"
medium
The rep did not verify or collect any contact details (phone/email) or the property address, so there is no way to follow up or send confirmation/details.
How to fix: Before ending the call, ask for name, phone, email, and property address; offer to text/email the inspection fee details and availability, and schedule a follow-up if they won’t book now.
"“Before you go, what’s your name and the property address? I can text you our earliest availability and the inspection details—what’s the best number/email?”"

Objections Detected (1)

medium Customer Needs Time to Decide Unresolved
"um okay. let me um so i'm calling around for prices um and let let me go ahead and just make a couple more calls and then um i can give you a call back if everything works out."
💡 Acknowledge the need to compare, then directly address it by (1) asking what quotes/criteria they’re comparing, (2) briefly framing the value/what the $3.99 fee covers, and (3) offering to lock in the soonest inspection slot (or a tentative hold) given their ASAP timeline: e.g., "Totally understand—since you need this ASAP for escrow, I can reserve our earliest opening while you compare. What day/time do you need the report by?"

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona referee, my name is Deva, how can I help you?
home_ownerhi, i am calling because i am in the process of um buying a home, it's currently in escrow and we just had a full inspection today and it looks like we're gonna need a roof inspection so i'm trying to see. And we just had a full inspection today, and it looks like we're gonna need a roof inspection, so I'm trying to… i can find anybody that could get out there like asap and help us out with that.
customer_repYeah, yeah, uh, I'm sure we can, I'm pretty sure that we have availability to get out there ASAP, um, for any real estate transactions we do charge a one time fee of $3.99 and that would go towards any repairs and or replacements depending on what you need, so if you're interested I can go ahead and. Call #19Joey Hallis+14805551234completedConfirmationWARM
62%
89% fields▼

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_afcae7545590
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments18

Summary

Joey Hallis called Arizona Rufus to speak with Travis Jones about a roof inspection appointment that had not been properly scheduled by the technician. Travis confirmed the appointment window and asked if the homeowner could still be available between 4:30 and 5:00, noting the technician was already on the way and might arrive a bit earlier. Joey agreed to wait at the property, and Travis said the technician would reach out. Joey also warned that calls from unknown numbers go to voicemail and requested the technician leave a voicemail message if they can’t get through.

• Customer (Joey Hallis) requested to speak with Travis Jones regarding a roof tech/inspection scheduling issue where the tech "dropped the ball" and didn’t get it scheduled.
• Travis confirmed the technician is on the way and the expected arrival window is between 4:30 and 5:00 (possibly a little sooner), and the homeowner agreed to stay and wait.
• Customer stated their phone sends calls from non-contacts to voicemail and asked that the technician leave a voice message if they can’t reach them.

Action Items

☑ Travis/team to have the technician call the customer upon arrival/when en route and leave a voicemail if the call goes to voicemail.
☑ Ensure the roof inspection appointment remains confirmed for the 4:30–5:00 arrival window (with possible earlier arrival).

Next Steps

➤ Technician arrives at the property between 4:30 and 5:00 (or slightly earlier) to perform the roof inspection.
➤ Customer waits on-site and checks voicemail for the technician’s message if the call is blocked by unknown-number screening.
Sentiment
62%
Summary Confidence
86%

SOP Compliance

62%
Followed (2/4)
Missed (2/4)
Greeting & IdentificationConfirm Appointment DetailsAnswer Questions (if any)Close Professionally

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
After the homeowner explained the scheduling issue and arrival window, the rep immediately transferred the call without confirming key appointment details (address/date/time window) or verifying the b
How to fix: Before transferring, quickly confirm the appointment window and the best number to reach the customer, plus any call-handling preferences (voicemail/text). Then warm-transfer with a concise recap to the manager.
"“Joey, I see the inspection is set for today between 4:30 and 5:00. Can I confirm the best phone number for the tech to call, and do you prefer a text or voicemail if it’s an unknown number? Great—let me bring Travis on and I’ll recap that for him.”"
low
During the transfer, the rep used an incorrect/unclear name, which could confuse the customer about who is on the line.
How to fix: Use the customer’s correct name (Joey) and clearly identify who is joining the call (Travis), then confirm the handoff.
"“Joey, I have Travis on the line now—Travis, Joey Hallis is calling to confirm the tech’s ETA between 4:30 and 5:00.”"
medium
The rep did not ensure the customer’s question/concern about unknown numbers going to voicemail was captured and addressed before the call ended (no follow-up or confirmation from the rep).
How to fix: When a customer flags a communication barrier, confirm the preferred method (voicemail/text) and document/relay it to the field team before ending the call.
"“Understood—since unknown numbers go to voicemail, I’ll note that the tech should leave a voicemail and, if possible, send a text as well. Is this the best number ending in ____?”"

Objections Detected (2)

medium Inefficient Agent Communication Unresolved
"In regards to the tech, the roof tech coming out to do the inspection on Sierra, he dropped the ball and didn't get it scheduled, and he wanted to know if they can, he's on his way, so he'll be here s"
💡 Directly address the service failure: apologize, confirm the correct appointment window, explain what happened briefly (if known), and state the corrective action (e.g., "I’m confirming the tech is en route for 4:30–5:00 and I’ll personally ensure you get an update if anything changes"). Then confirm the customer is satisfied with the fix.
low Immediate Service Unavailability Unresolved
"and, uh. Yeah. I will, I'm not sure what number he's going to call on because it's, if I don't, I have my phone set up that if you're not a contact, it automatically goes to voicemail."
💡 Offer a concrete communication plan: confirm the best callback number, ask permission to text, provide the tech’s caller ID if possible, or instruct the customer to add the number as a contact. Then repeat back the plan (e.g., "He’ll leave a voicemail and I’ll also text you when he’s 10 minutes out").

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Rufus. This is Matty. How can I help you?
home_ownerHello, Matty. Can I speak to Travis Jones again? This is Joey Hallis.
customer_repyeah, in regards to what?
home_ownerIn regards to the tech, the roof tech coming out to do the inspection on Sierra, he dropped the ball and didn't get it scheduled, and he wanted to know if they can, he's on his way, so he'll be here sometime between 4.30 and 5.00.
customer_repyeah yeah i do see that here on the calendar let me go ahead and get you over to travis.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repalright julia i have travis in the line for you now.
home_ownerHello, Travis.
managerAlright. All right. so. We can hang out here. Can you guys still make it sometime between 4.30 and 5?00? he is on his way, he might be a little bit sooner than that, would that be okay?
home_ownerOkay, that's fine. We'll stay here and wait for them.
manageri appreciate it and i'll have them reach out to you okay?
home_owneralright.
managerand i'll have them reach out to you, okay?
home_ownerokay, appreciate it, yeah.

managerOh, that's fair. It might not work then.
home_ownerOh, that's fair. It might not work then. Tell him to leave a, tell him to leave a voice message. I do. Yeah. Ah.
managerThank you so much, Travis. Bye-bye.

Call Details

Call IDreport_test_a69e113b04ea
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments6

Summary

Cindy Hannah called Arizona Roofers to return a call from Kate. The customer service representative, Bronte, informed Cindy that Kate was not in yet and said she would let Kate know that Cindy called when she arrived. The call ended politely without any service details discussed or an appointment scheduled.

• Caller identified herself as Cindy Hannah and said she was returning Kate's call.
• Customer service rep Bronte stated that Kate was not in yet.
• Bronte said she would let Kate know that Cindy called when Kate is available; no roofing issue or appointment was discussed.

Action Items

☑ Notify Kate that Cindy Hannah returned her call.
☑ Have Kate follow up with Cindy Hannah to continue the conversation.
☑ Inform Kate that Cindy called when Kate arrives

Next Steps

➤ Kate to call Cindy Hannah back when she is in.
➤ If contact is made, gather details on the roofing need and property specifics to determine appropriate service and scheduling.
Sentiment
75%
Summary Confidence
90%

SOP Compliance

40%

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
After the homeowner said they were returning Kate's call, the rep only stated Kate wasn't in yet and that they would let her know, without confirming the caller's best callback number, spelling/last n
How to fix: Acknowledge the callback, then capture/confirm key details: full name, best callback number, and what the call is regarding (project/address or job type). Document and route the message to the correct person/team.
"“Absolutely—I can help with that. Can I get your last name and the best number for Kate to call you back? And is this regarding your roof project at [address]?”"
medium
The rep did not set any expectation for when Kate would be available or when the customer should expect a return call, and did not offer an alternative (voicemail, another team member, or scheduling a
How to fix: Provide a clear timeframe and options: estimated return-call window, offer to take a detailed message, or offer to connect with another available person if urgent.
"“Kate should be in by around [time]. I’ll message her right now—when is the best time today for her to call you back? If it’s urgent, I can also see who’s available to help right away.”"
low
The rep closed the call without checking if the homeowner had any immediate questions or concerns that could be addressed in the meantime.
How to fix: Before ending, ask an open-ended question to surface needs and either resolve them or route appropriately.
"“Before you go, is there anything I can help with today while we wait for Kate—any questions about the next steps or your project?”"

Segments52

Summary

The homeowner called Arizona Roofers to ask about pricing and to schedule an on-site estimate for replacing an older shingle roof that is leaking despite having been patched before. The representative confirmed the roof type (shingle), collected the property address, name, phone number, permission to send text updates, and an email for sending a written proposal, and noted the roof is around 20 years old with no solar panels (customer mentioned an air conditioner on the roof). The customer expressed concern about prior poor installation and requested certified technicians; the representative reassured them that shingle roofs commonly begin leaking around 15–20 years and that the company’s team is certified and licensed. An appointment was booked for Thursday from 1–3 PM, with confirmation and pre-arrival communication via text/phone.

• Customer has a shingle roof and is requesting an estimate for a roof replacement due to leaking; they mentioned the roof is around 20 years old and has been patched before.
• Property details captured: address provided (1301 West El Nary/Elmarae Street, unit/apt details unclear), homeowner confirmed they are the owner, best contact is the current phone number, text updates approved, and email provided for the written proposal.
• Customer stated there are no solar panels and mentioned an air conditioner on the roof; they also emphasized wanting a certified technician because a prior roof installation began leaking within about five years.

Action Items

☑ Send appointment confirmation text and provide day-of text/phone notification about 30 minutes before technician arrival.
☑ Prepare to email the written proposal/estimate to the customer after the on-site visit.

Next Steps

➤ Technician to visit the property on Thursday from 1–3 PM to inspect the shingle roof and provide a replacement estimate.
➤ After inspection, deliver the written proposal to the customer via email and follow up as needed to proceed with replacement.
Sentiment
72%
Summary Confidence
78%

SOP Compliance

74%
Followed (4/7)
Missed (3/7)
Greeting & IdentificationSchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & ConfirmationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)Objection Handling

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
After the customer said they have leaking and want an estimate to replace the roof, the rep moved quickly into intake and scheduling without clarifying the problem details (how long leaking has been h
How to fix: Before scheduling, ask 3–5 targeted discovery questions to assess urgency and scope (duration, active leak, interior damage, prior patch/inspection, roof type/sections affected). Then route appropriately (standard inspection vs. urgent leak service).
"“Thanks—so I can get you scheduled correctly: Is it actively leaking right now? How long has the leak been going on? Have you had anyone look at it or done any repairs/patching? Where are you seeing the water inside the home?”"
medium
The rep did not complete BANT-style qualifying beyond confirming homeowner status; they did not ask about timeline/urgency for replacement, whether the customer is comparing other bids, or any budget/
How to fix: Add quick qualifying questions after identifying the need and before locking the appointment: timeline, decision process, and whether they’re getting other quotes; optionally mention financing if appropriate.
"“When are you hoping to get this taken care of—ASAP or are you planning for later? Are you getting a couple other quotes as well? And will anyone else need to be involved in the decision?”"
high
When the customer raised a trust/certification concern, the rep responded with potentially inaccurate reassurance (“totally normal… to start leaking after about 15 to 20 years”) and vague credibility
How to fix: Acknowledge the concern, clarify what ‘certified’ means (manufacturer certifications, state license, insurance), and set expectations that the technician will document findings and explain options. Avoid absolute claims; use verifiable facts and offer to provide license numbers/links.
"“I understand why you’d want a certified installer after that experience. Our technicians are licensed and insured, and we can share our license information. At the visit, the tech will inspect the roof, take photos, and walk you through what caused the leak and the best repair vs. replacement options.”"

Objections Detected (3)

medium Other Unresolved
"I have a question, ma'. Am. I like to have recorded the lock tie before you do the roof. I don't know, you do something wrong or what. So I need to certify the technician to install to take a look and"
💡 Acknowledge the fear of repeat problems, then directly explain the process: inspection steps, how findings are documented (photos/video), whether the customer can record, what certifications the installer holds, and what warranty/quality checks prevent recurrence; then confirm: "Does that address your concern about having proof and avoiding another leak?"
low In-Person Estimates Only Unresolved
"So I want to know this. How much cost? Can somebody can stop by and take a look to see how much cost?"
💡 Directly address the pricing question: explain that an accurate estimate requires an on-site inspection (measurements, decking condition, number of layers, ventilation, flashing, etc.), give a rough range if allowed, confirm whether the inspection/estimate is free, and then schedule the visit.
low Service Fee Concerns Unresolved
"I already have a pack over but I want to estimate if good deal."
💡 Clarify whether the estimate/inspection has a fee and what it includes. If free, explicitly say so. If there is a fee, justify it with what’s included (inspection, photos, written proposal) and apply/credit it toward the job if applicable, then confirm the customer is comfortable proceeding.

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Roofers. This is Bronte. How can I help you?
home_ownerOh, hi. I check on the website and see your guy have a certified roofing installation for single roofing.

customer_repOkay.
home_ownerYeah, I have a linking. So I want to know this. How much cost? Can somebody can stop by and take a look to see how much cost? I do replay the new one.

customer_repOkay.

customer_repOkay. And are you the homeowner?

home_ownerSo I know.
customer_repOkay. And do you have any solar panels on the roof?

customer_repOkay.
home_ownerI have air condition on the roof.
customer_repOkay. All right. I can have somebody out to you this Thursday, February 12th at 1pm does that work for you?
home_ownerThursday? Okay.
customer_repOkay. You said one o'. Clock. One to 3pm does that work for you?
home_ownerOkay. Yeah, one to three. Fine.
customer_repOkay, I'll book that for you. And then once we're off this phone call, you'll get a confirmation text with the appointment details. And then the day of your appointment, your technician will give you a text or a phone call when he's about 30 minutes away from the property so you know exactly when to expect him.
home_ownerI have a question, ma'. Am. I like to have recorded the lock tie before you do the roof. I don't know, you do something wrong or what. So I need to certify the technician to install to take a look and see what he suggest. I don't want that have problem again.
customer_repWhat do you mean?
home_ownerOkay, the rope people installed for me before 30 years. But the fifth year they already have a leaking. So maybe when the people before you do something not correct.
customer_repNo, that's normal for that's totally normal for shingle roofs to start leaking after about 15 to 20 years.
home_ownerOkay.
customer_repYeah. And everyone here is certified. We're licensed under the bbb. If you'd like to look us up at the Better Business Bureau, you can. We're all licensed.
home_ownerOkay. I like to certify people sometimes not. I'm going to take the look or something. I like certified people. Donation come to take. The locality will do the best for my rule.
customer_repYeah, we've. We've been in business for about 28 years, so no one's going to steer you wrong.
home_ownerOkay.
customer_repAll right. So we'll be on Thursday from 1 to 3?
home_ownerYep.
customer_repAnd they'll give you a text before they arrive?
home_ownerSure. Thank you very much, ma'. Am.
customer_repOf course. Have a good day, June.

customer_repBye. Bye. Bye. Bye.

Call Details

Call IDstaging_run_74aaf9a76502
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments48

Summary

Jennifer Barker from Optimum/Optical Medical called Arizona Roofers to request a roof inspection for a commercial property they own at 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ, which is being converted from a former bank into a primary care clinic. She reported that contractors recently found a couple of roof leaks over the last few days and asked if someone could inspect as soon as tomorrow (2026-03-03). Steve explained he could not guarantee a next-day visit but took down the property and contact details, confirmed the building is single-story with a flat roof, and noted there are no known gate codes. He advised that a technician will coordinate the inspection time/date with her, and that the inspection includes a full photo set and report with options and proposals sent via email; Jennifer also opted to receive text updates. The call ended with the rep committing to have the technician reach out to schedule.

• Customer requested a roof inspection for a commercial property in Surprise due to a couple of roof leaks found over the last few days and asked for availability tomorrow (2026-03-03).
• Property details provided: 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ 85374; single-story building; flat roof; no known gate codes; roof age unknown.
• Outcome: Rep could not guarantee a next-day appointment, collected Jennifer Barker’s contact info and confirmed a technician will reach out to coordinate the inspection time/date; customer prefers text message updates and proposals via email.

Action Items

☑ Assign/notify a technician to contact Jennifer Barker to schedule the roof inspection for the Surprise commercial property.
☑ Send scheduling confirmations/updates via text and deliver proposals/report to Jennifer’s email after the inspection.

Next Steps

➤ Technician contacts Jennifer to set an inspection appointment date/time (requested as soon as 2026-03-03, but not guaranteed).
➤ Ensure someone is on site (contractors) during the inspection as requested by the company.

SOP Compliance

Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT-lite)Setting ExpectationsClose (partial)Scheduling (no appointment set/confirmed)Close & Confirmation (no confirmed date/time; no confirmation message recap)Objection Handling (not applicable—no objections raised)

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After the caller requested an inspection 'tomorrow,' the rep did not offer available time windows or book a firm appointment; instead, he said he would pass the info to a technician to coordinate late
How to fix: Attempt to schedule on the call by offering concrete time windows and confirming who will be on-site. If the rep truly cannot schedule, set a clear SLA for the technician callback and confirm the next step.
"“I can’t guarantee tomorrow yet, but I can offer two windows: 9–11am or 1–3pm. Which works best? If neither works, I’ll have our technician call you within the next 30 minutes to lock a time—what’s the best number to reach you?”"
medium
The rep collected address, email, and phone but did not read them back for confirmation and did not confirm the preferred communication method beyond a brief consent question.
How to fix: After collecting details, repeat them back and ask for confirmation, then summarize next steps and timing for follow-up.
"“Let me confirm: 13926 W Bell Rd, Surprise, AZ 85374, and your cell is 909-684-6098, email jbarker@optimal… Is that all correct? Great—expect a text update and a call from our technician by [time].”"
low
At the start, the rep responded with a confused/abrupt interruption when the caller referenced an earlier email, which created unnecessary friction before needs discovery.
How to fix: Acknowledge the email, confirm the inbox succinctly, and transition smoothly into the request and urgency.
"“Thanks for emailing us—was that to info@arizonaroofers.com? Perfect. How can we help at the Surprise location, and how urgent are the leaks?”"
medium
Needs discovery did not include key urgency/damage questions (how long leaking, active water intrusion, interior damage, safety concerns) before moving into intake and logistics.
How to fix: Add 2–3 triage questions immediately after the customer reports leaks, then proceed to property details and scheduling.
"“How long has the leaking been happening? Is it actively leaking right now, and is there any interior ceiling or electrical impact? Any areas we should prioritize when we arrive?”"

Transcript (48 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Roofers. My name is Steve. How can I help you out?
home_ownerMy name is Jennifer Barker. I'm with Optical Medical, and I sent an email a little bit earlier today. I have a site enterprise that
customer_repWhat? Oh. You send an email to… You think- What, you sent the email to what email, sorry? The email to what email first?
home_ownerThe info at Arizona Roofers.
customer_repOkay.
home_ownerI have a location in surprise that we are currently in process of making into a clinic, and over the last couple of days, they found some roof leaks, and I'm looking to see if you guys have somebody available tomorrow that can do an inspection for me.
customer_repDo you know how old the roof is?
home_ownerI am. no idea. And that's a… Unfortunately, I have very, I have very little information about the, about the, um, the age of the roof or anything like that.
customer_repOkay, and this is a commercial property.
home_ownerIt is.
customer_repOkay, I'm not sure if I can guarantee someone coming out there tomorrow, um, but we could take your information down and I can give it to my technician and he can coordinate a time and date, um, MS, do you own the property or are you a project?
home_ownerWe do own it. We do own it, yes.
customer_repGotcha. And what type of a clinic is it?
home_ownerWell, it's a primary care medical clinic. Okay. But it was, it was a bank and we're converting it into a clinic.
customer_repGotcha, and you're just experiencing that, those couple of leaks on-
home_ownerYes.
customer_repOkay, let me grab your information down and then I will have a technician reach out to you. Um, can I start with the property address?
home_ownerSure, it's one, three, nine, two, six, West Bell Road, in surprise, and it's eight, five, three, seven, four.
customer_repNo. You're gonna be the point of-
home_ownerI am the main contact, yes. I will not be on Facebook.
customer_repOkay, can I get yours? Okay, is someone gonna be on site? We do require someone to be on site during time of inspection, preferably.
home_ownerI believe I believe we do have, um, our contractors there. You have, um, our contractors there.
customer_repOkay, you don't have any decision makers that aren't gonna be able to be on site, like any owners or managers or anything like that?
home_ownerNo, not that I'm aware of, no.
customer_repOkay. Okay. Can I grab your first and last name?
home_ownerSure. It's Jennifer Barker, and it's B. S. A. R. K. E. R. .
customer_repAnd Jennifer, what's your role there? Are you, uh, just… Okay. And so… Thank you. here. Okay, perfect. Can I grab your email, Jennifer?
home_ownerSure, it's J. Barker, again, B. S. P. L. A. R. K. E. R., at optimal. After… That's O. P. is in Paul. T. is in Tom. I. M. A. Mm-hmm. A is in apple, Z is in zebra dot com.
customer_repOkay. Then the- The last phone number to reach you?
home_ownerUh, my cell phone is nine zero nine six eight four six zero nine eight.
customer_repOkay. Am I able to send you email and text message updates about the roof?
home_ownerText message update. Yeah.
customer_repOkay. Do you know what type of roofing material is on the roof?
home_ownerTile should color flat. Uh, it's a flat roof. But okay.
customer_repIs this a single story, uh, building?
home_ownerYes. Mm-hmm.
customer_repDo you know how big it is, square feet-wise?
home_ownerI don't have information straight off the end, but I can probably figure it out, but I don't have it with me.
customer_repOkay, no worries. Um, is there any gate codes we should be aware of?
home_ownerand not that i'm aware of, no.
customer_repOkay, I'm assuming there's no HOA since it's commercial, and who do, who owns the property?
home_ownerUh, Optimum does.
customer_repAnd then I'm assuming we're gonna be sending, so we'll have the technician coordinate with you and then still be sending all that information to your email, like proposals and stuff.
home_ownerMm-hmm.
customer_repOkay. All right, I'll go ahead and let my technician know, and he'll reach out with you with the time and day that works for both of you. Um, how it kind of works is when they come out there, you'll be receiving, uh, when they come out here, they do a full roof inspection, they take about fifty to a hundred photos, we write a full report, um, with all of your options, and then I know if you guys choose to go with the same day, they do take some money off the top as well. But if you don't have any other questions, Jennifer, I, you'll be getting reached out to,
home_ownerNow, I appreciate that. Thank you so much.
customer_repOf course, no problem. Have a good day.
home_ownerYou too.

Statuscompleted
Segments16

Summary

Joey Hallis called Arizona Rufus asking to speak with Travis Jones about a roof inspection appointment on Sierra, explaining that the roof tech had not scheduled it but was now on the way. The customer service rep confirmed the appointment on the calendar and transferred the call to Travis. Travis confirmed the technician could arrive between 4:30 and 5:00 (possibly a bit earlier), and Joey agreed to wait at the property. Travis said the technician would reach out, and Joey noted that unknown numbers go to voicemail on his phone, requesting that the technician leave a voicemail message if he can’t get through.

• Caller Joey Hallis requested to speak with Travis Jones regarding a roof tech inspection on Sierra that hadn’t been scheduled as expected.
• Travis confirmed the technician is en route and expected to arrive between 4:30 and 5:00 (possibly earlier), and the customer agreed to wait.
• Customer’s phone sends calls from non-contacts to voicemail; customer asked that the technician leave a voicemail message if the call doesn’t connect.

Action Items

☑ Ensure the roof technician calls the customer upon arrival/when en route and leaves a voicemail if the call goes to voicemail.
☑ Confirm internally that the inspection appointment on Sierra is properly scheduled and reflected on the calendar.
☑ Transfer Joey Hallis to Travis Jones on the current call

Next Steps

➤ Technician arrives at the Sierra location between 4:30 and 5:00 on 2026-03-02 (or slightly earlier) to perform the inspection.
➤ Technician contacts Joey Hallis before/at arrival and leaves a voicemail if unable to reach him live.

Greeting & Identification (partial)Transfer/route to requested personConfirm Appointment DetailsClose Professionally

AI Coaching Recommendations (2)

medium
After the homeowner explained the appointment scheduling issue and time window, the rep immediately transferred the call without confirming the appointment details or verifying the best callback numbe
How to fix: Before transferring (or while placing the caller on hold), quickly recap and confirm the appointment window and the best number to reach them, plus any call-screening/voicemail instructions. Then transfer with a clear warm handoff.
"“Joey, I see the inspection for Sierra today. Just to confirm, the tech is expected between 4:30 and 5:00—does that still work? And what’s the best number for the tech to call? If they’re not in your contacts, should they leave a voicemail or text?”"
low
During the transfer, the rep used an unexpected/incorrect name, which can confuse the caller about who is on the line and whether the transfer was successful.
How to fix: Use the caller’s correct name (or avoid names if unsure) and clearly state who is being connected. If uncertain of the name, ask or confirm it first.
"“Joey, I have Travis on the line now—one moment while I connect you.”"

Objections Detected (2)

medium Inefficient Agent Communication Resolved
"In regards to the tech, the roof tech coming out to do the inspection on Sierra, he dropped the ball and didn't get it scheduled, and he wanted to know if they can, he's on his way, so he'll be here s"
💡 Best practice: acknowledge the scheduling miss, apologize, confirm the exact appointment window, and set clear next steps (who will call/text and when) to rebuild confidence.
low Inefficient Agent Communication Unresolved
"Yeah. I will, I'm not sure what number he's going to call on because it's, if I don't, I have my phone set up that if you're not a contact, it automatically goes to voicemail."
💡 Per guideline #1 (directly address): propose and confirm a specific contact method (e.g., 'We’ll have the tech text you first from X number' or 'I’ll note to leave a voicemail and also send a text'), then get explicit customer agreement.

Transcript (16 segments)

customer_repThank you for calling Arizona Rufus. This is Matty. How can I help you?
home_ownerHello, Matty. Can I speak to Travis Jones again? This is Joey Hallis.
customer_repyeah, in regards to what?
home_ownerIn regards to the tech, the roof tech coming out to do the inspection on Sierra, he dropped the ball and didn't get it scheduled, and he wanted to know if they can, he's on his way, so he'll be here sometime between 4.30 and 5.00.
customer_repyeah yeah i do see that here on the calendar let me go ahead and get you over to travis.
home_ownerokay.
customer_repalright julia i have travis in the line for you now.
home_ownerHello, Travis.
managerAlright. All right. so. We can hang out here. Can you guys still make it sometime between 4.30 and 5?00? he is on his way, he might be a little bit sooner than that, would that be okay?
home_ownerOkay, that's fine. We'll stay here and wait for them.
manageri appreciate it and i'll have them reach out to you okay?
home_owneralright.
managerand i'll have them reach out to you, okay?
home_ownerokay, appreciate it, yeah. and, uh. Yeah. I will, I'm not sure what number he's going to call on because it's, if I don't, I have my phone set up that if you're not a contact, it automatically goes to voicemail.
managerOh, that's fair. It might not work then. Oh, that's fair. It might not work then.
home_ownerTell him to leave a, tell him to leave a voice message. I do. Yeah. Ah. Thank you so much, Travis. Bye-bye.
