Call Details

Call IDtest-fix-flores-001
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone4804535721
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments6

Summary

Cindy Hannah called Arizona Roofers to return a call from Kate. Bronte informed Cindy that Kate was not in yet and said she would let Kate know that Cindy called when she arrived. The call ended politely without discussing any roofing issue, property details, or scheduling.

• Caller identified herself as Cindy Hannah and said she was returning Kate's call.
• Customer rep Bronte stated Kate was not in yet.
• Bronte said she would let Kate know Cindy called when Kate is there; no appointment or service details were discussed.

Next Steps

➤ Kate to follow up with Cindy Hannah after receiving the message.
➤ If needed, Kate to clarify the reason for the call and determine whether any roofing service is requested.
Sentiment
75%
Summary Confidence
90%

SOP Compliance

30%
Followed (1/6)
Missed (5/6)
Greeting & IdentificationNeeds DiscoveryQualifying (BANT)SchedulingSetting ExpectationsClose & Confirmation

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

high
When the caller said they were returning Kate's call, the rep only stated Kate wasn't in and ended the call without gathering a callback number, email, or details about what Kate called about.
How to fix: Use a brief message-taking workflow: confirm the caller’s full name, best callback number, address (if relevant), and what the call is regarding; then confirm when/ how Kate will return the call.
"“I can help with that—can I get your best callback number and the address on the account? Also, do you know what Kate was calling about (repair, inspection, estimate)? I’ll leave her a detailed message and have her call you back today.”"
medium
After learning the requested person was unavailable, the rep did not offer any alternative path (transfer to another rep, scheduling an appointment, or taking a detailed message with a promised callba
How to fix: Offer immediate alternatives: check if another team member can assist, offer to schedule an inspection/estimate, or set a specific callback time window.
"“Kate isn’t in yet—would you like me to connect you with someone who can help now, or I can schedule a quick inspection/estimate while I have you? If you prefer Kate, what’s a good time window for her to call you back?”"
medium
The call ended without confirming next steps (who will call back, expected timing, and confirmation of the caller’s contact method).
How to fix: Close with a clear commitment and confirmation: restate the action, timeframe, and how the customer will be contacted; confirm the best number.
"“Perfect—I'll message Kate right now. She’ll call you back this morning. Just to confirm, is (###-###-####) the best number to reach you?”"

Segments6

Summary

Cindy Hannah called Arizona Roofers to return a call from Kate. Bronte informed her that Kate was not in yet and said she would let Kate know that Cindy called when she arrived. The call ended politely without discussing any roofing issues, property details, or scheduling.

• Caller identified herself as Cindy Hannah and said she was returning Kate's call.
• Customer service rep Bronte stated Kate was not in yet.
• Bronte said she would let Kate know Cindy called when Kate is in.

Action Items

☑ Notify Kate that Cindy Hannah returned her call.
☑ Have Kate follow up with Cindy Hannah when available.
☑ Notify Kate that Cindy called back when Kate arrives

Next Steps

➤ Kate contacts Cindy Hannah to continue the conversation.
➤ Determine the reason for the original outreach and any roofing service needs during the follow-up.

AI Coaching Recommendations (3)

medium
After the caller said she was returning Kate's call, the rep only stated Kate was not in yet and ended the interaction without confirming who the caller is (full name), the best callback number, or wh
How to fix: Acknowledge the return call, confirm the caller's full name, verify the best callback number, and capture a brief reason for the call so the intended rep can respond effectively.
"“Thanks, Cindy. Can I get your last name and the best phone number for Kate to reach you? And just so I can help her, what was Kate calling you about—was it regarding your roof project or a scheduling update?”"
medium
The rep did not set any expectation for when Kate would return the call (no timeframe, no promise to call back today, no alternative contact method).
How to fix: Provide a realistic callback window and offer an alternative (voicemail, direct line, or taking a message) if the customer needs faster assistance.
"“Kate should be in by [time window]. I’ll message her that you called, and she’ll return your call today. If you’d like, I can also take a quick message for her right now.”"
low
Before ending the call, the rep did not ask if the caller had any questions or urgent concerns that needed immediate help while Kate was unavailable.
How to fix: Add a quick check for urgency and offer immediate assistance or escalation if needed.
"“Before you go—was there anything urgent you needed help with today, or any quick question I can answer while Kate is out?”"

Call Details

Call IDstaging_run_3a473c9412d3
Company ID1be5ea90-d3ae-4b03-8b05-f5679cd73bc4
Phone+14805551234
DateNone
Statuscompleted
Segments10

Summary

Maria Ramirez called Arizona Rufus/Arizona Roofer and indicated she does not speak Spanish. The representative attempted to coordinate communication (mentioning Camilla) and confirmed they were speaking with Maria Ramirez. The rep then stated they would request a callback with the production team, and Maria agreed, ending the call without discussing any specific roofing issue or scheduling details.

• Caller (Maria Ramirez) stated she does not speak Spanish.
• The representative confirmed the caller’s name as Maria Ramirez.
• The representative said they would request a callback with the production/production team, and the caller agreed.

Action Items

☑ Submit a request for the production team to call back Maria Ramirez.
☑ Ensure the callback is handled in English (caller stated she does not speak Spanish).

Next Steps

➤ Production team to call Maria Ramirez back to determine the reason for the call and needed roofing service.
➤ During the callback, collect property and access details (roof issue, address, roof type, stories, HOA/gate/pets/solar) since none were captured on this call.
Sentiment
60%
Summary Confidence
78%

SOP Compliance

32%
Followed (1/5)
Missed (4/5)
Greeting & IdentificationConfirm Project DetailsSet Expectations for Next StepsAddress Questions/ConcernsClose Professionally

AI Coaching Recommendations (4)

high
After the customer indicated a language mismatch ('no, i don't speak spanish'), the rep did not clarify language preference or reset the conversation; instead, the rep continued with unclear phrasing
How to fix: Acknowledge the language issue, confirm the preferred language, and either transfer to an appropriate agent/translator or continue in the confirmed language with clear, simple questions.
"“Thanks for letting me know. Do you prefer English? I can help you in English, or I can connect you with a Spanish-speaking teammate if you prefer. Which would you like?”"
high
The rep did not confirm project details (what the customer needs, job status, address, or any reference number) before stating they would request a callback from production.
How to fix: Before routing, confirm at minimum: customer name, best callback number, address/job identifier, and the specific reason for the production callback.
"“To make sure production helps you quickly, can I confirm your address and the best callback number? And what question do you have for production—schedule, materials, or something else?”"
medium
The rep did not set expectations for next steps—no timeframe for the callback, no confirmation of contact method, and no guidance on what will happen next.
How to fix: Provide a clear callback window, confirm the phone number, and explain what the production team will cover on the call.
"“I’m submitting this to production now. You should receive a call within the next 24 hours at the number we have on file. They’ll review your project status and answer your questions.”"
medium
The rep did not close professionally by confirming the request, checking for final questions, or offering a way to reach the company if the callback is missed.
How to fix: Summarize the action taken, ask if there’s anything else, and provide a simple next step if the customer doesn’t hear back.
"“Just to confirm, I’ve requested a production callback for you. Is there anything else I can help with today? If you don’t hear from us by tomorrow, please call us back at this number and we’ll escalate it.”"

Objections Detected (1)

medium Other Unresolved
"no, i don't speak spanish, i do apologize."
💡 Best practice: directly confirm the language preference and adapt immediately (e.g., "No problem—we can continue in English. Would you prefer English, or should I connect you with a Spanish-speaking rep?") and get explicit confirmation before moving forward.

Transcript (10 segments)

customer_repHi, this is Lisa from Arizona Rufus. How can I help?
customer_rephi, this is from arizona rooper, how can i help?
home_ownerno, i don't speak spanish, i do apologize.

SPEAKER_00Thank you for calling Arizona Roofers. My name is Diva. How can I help you? Hi, this is Alciana. I'm a real estate agent out here in Arizona. I have a client that is looking to purchase a property and the roof is pretty. In pretty rough condition, but we would like to get a quote on it just to make sure. Just to see how much it would be to fix it. Is that something that you guys could do? Yeah, of course. Okay, we do. Are you guys in the contract stage or. Yes. Okay, perfect. So I'm assuming you have a timeline when you need to have that. Oh my. You're on a timeline? Yes. As soon as you guys would be able to do it. Gotcha. I could take a look at my schedule here. So just a heads up, for real estate transactions, we do charge a one time fee of 3.99 to have a technician come out and give you a full estimate, full report. They take about 15, 100 photos, write up a report like the whole thing. So it was $399. And then that fee goes towards 399. 399. It goes towards any repairs and or replacements if you guys choose to go with us as well. So if you're interested, we can go ahead and get that scheduled for you. Okay. I'll have to check with my clients to see if they're okay with the 300 fee because it'll be them that had to take care of it. Okay. Yeah, yeah. I'll get back to you guys. Thank you so much. Sounds good. Have a good day. Same to you. Bye.

SPEAKER_00For calling Arizona roofers. My name is Diva. How can I help you? Hi, Diva. I actually was in conversation with Maddie regarding the plan for repairing my roof. So do you know if she's still around or is it too late to. Yeah, let me. Let me put you on a brief hold and see if she's available and I'll get you transferred over if she is. Okay. Okay, thank you. Yeah. One moment, please. Stay on the line. Sa. Alrighty, you're all transferred. Awesome. Thank you. Hey, Ash, how are you? Hey, Maddie. Thanks. I'm good, thanks. Sorry, I know we were trying to connect previously, but I. Oh, no, you're fine. You're fine. Are you all done with work now? Yeah, I can. I can talk now. I have my 100% attention. Yeah, yeah, no, I can. I can talk to you for a sec here. Just let you know what to expect for the first phase of your project. It really shouldn't take very long. Okay. All right. So the first phase of your project here with us is what we call the preparation phase. This is just where your file will go through review by one of our managers. And what they're going to do is they're going to verify things like your measurements, materials, and photos of your roof, as well as the scope of work for your job and colors. That's just what helps us make sure that your project gets over to our production team smoothly. And then if there's any extra information that we need from you, we might reach out to get that from you. So just keep an eye out for any additional phone calls from us. And then once this manager review here is completed, that's where your job moves over to the pre production phase. And it's just for our production team. They're going to start doing things like building out your material orders, work orders, as well as completing the crew assignment for your job. And they'll also complete any color or material selection forms and logistical planning. And then once all of this pre production stuff is done, that's when you can expect that phone call from our production team just to get your availability for your project as well as go over what to expect when you start your roof install for you. Do you have any questions about that, Ash? Yes. When is day one? Basically, when does the project manager come here and we get started with the process? Yeah, so the production team will give you a phone call in the next five or seven business days just to go over that availability with you. Because we can't really get you scheduled until we know if there's, you know, Certain days you can't do the project, but we can't call you for scheduling until we get your materials ordered and get a crew figured out for, like, who's going to go out there and do your job. But once they call you for that availability and figure out if there's any dates, you can't do it. And then they'll also go over your material delivery with you, let you know what happens with that. And they will just go over what to expect during the job, like how long the job will take, what you should expect to be going on, and stuff like that. And after they get that all squared away with you, a few days after that, they'll give you that call to give you your official date. Okay. Yeah, there's just a lot that goes into getting everything ready to get scheduled. Yeah. Right. Right. Okay. Yeah. I would prefer to expedite this as soon as possible, as much as possible. And there are no blocker dates for. From my thing, so just keep that in mind. Or if you have to put that in notes, please do so and then. Yeah, I look forward to the call. Alrighty. Ash, do you have anything else for me? No, I'm good. Okay. Well, if you do have any other questions or need anything, you can call me anytime. Great. Thank you. Yeah, you're welcome. I hope you have a great day. You too. Bye. Bye.
